handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086), _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_, [Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77) (20 arrows), beaded earrings (worth 20 gp) --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +15 **HP** 120 ([coffin restoration](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480), [fast healing](/Rules.aspx?ID=2322) 10, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))) **Immunities** [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** all physical 10 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) or weapons shedding [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402)) **Weaknesses** [strigoi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=314) (revulsion, sunlight, water revulsion) --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d10+10 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** shortbow +19 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Divine Innate Spell** DC 25 - **5th** [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584) (at will) **Drink Essence** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or willing creature is within the strigoi's reach; **Effect** Okoa sinks her fangs into the targeted creature to drink its blood and draw out its vital essence. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the Fortitude DC if the creature is grabbed and is automatic for any other conditions. The creature becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1, and Okoa regains 13 Hit Points, gaining any excess Hit Points as temporary Hit Points. Drinking Essence from a creature that's already drained or stupefied doesn't restore any Hit Points, but increases the creature's drained condition value or its stupefied condition value by 1 (whichever value is lesser is increased; if both values are equal, then the strigoi chooses which condition to increase the value of).
A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a successful DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drained value by 1 after 10 minutes. A victim's stupefied condition decreases by 1 per day after performing daily preparations. If the daily preparations are done in full sunlight, the stupefied condition is removed entirely. **Shadow Form** (concentrate, divine, shadow) Okoa reverts to pure shadow and absorbs her body and her gear into the darkness, or it shifts back to her physical form. In shadow form, Okoa gains a climb Speed equal to her land Speed and can more through any gap that isn't airtight. However, she can only move along solid surfaces that aren't highly reflective, not liquid or mirrored surfaces. If the surface she's on is destroyed, Okoa returns to physical form and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. She loses fast healing while in shadow form but can remain in shadow form indefinitely.
If Okoa is exposed to sunlight while in shadow form, she becomes slowed 2 and must attempt a DC 16 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) at the end of each of her turns. If she fails, she is destroyed, the shadow vanishing with a blood-curdling wail. **Strafing Strike** Okoa becomes a semi-incorporeal shade, then Strides or Flies up to twice her Speed in a straight line, dealing 8d8 slashing damage to all creatures in that line or adjacent to that line (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). She can move through enemies and solid objects during this movement. At the end of this movement, she returns to her fully corporeal form. She can't use Strafing Strike again for 1d4 rounds. **Vicious Flurry** ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=606)) Okoa makes two claw Strikes at the same target, each using her current multiple attack penalty. If both attacks hit, the target is also dealt 1d8 [persistent bleed damage.](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)
name: Okoa

**Construct Armor** Like normal objects, an animated treasure swarm has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage the swarm takes by an amount equal to its Hardness. Once an animated treasure swarm is reduced to fewer than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon becoming damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its AC to 21.

**One with the Hoard** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The animated treasure swarm is adjacent to Viseldron's hoard; **Effect** The animated treasure swarm enters Viseldron's hoard, then emerges from the hoard at any other point in, or adjacent to, the hoard's area.

**Trash the Place** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) The animated treasure swarm hurls its most fragile components in a destructive barrage, dealing 4d6 bludgeoning damage to all creatures in a 20- foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 100 feet (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

</column>

markdown:
Aeon stone swarms are constructs composed of hundreds of magical gems called [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3055) that orbit around a central point in a tumultuous, evershifting mass. Each swarm is imbued with intelligence and composed of a collection of _aeon stones_, which can arrange themselves in a variety of configurations to activate unique resonant powers. While most aeon stone swarms adopt similar configurations, it's possible to create swarms with experimental structures, resulting in different or additional resonant powers. Many aeon stone swarms are created to protect a particular location clandestinely, with the swarm using its subterfuge resonance to disguise itself as a mosaic of precious stones or a pile of gemstones lying in an open chest. Unfortunately for would-be thieves, nearly all the aeon stones composing an aeon stone swarm shatter when the swarm is destroyed. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Aeon Stone Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3891) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 82 **Perception** +15; darkvision **Languages** [Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49); (can’t speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +21 **Will** +16 **HP** 110 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** piercing 10, slashing 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash damage](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) 10 **Aeon Interference** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 20 feet. An aeon stone swarm is surrounded by a pall of magical interference, which hampers spellcasting in the area. Whenever a creature in the aura attempts to [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=3271), they must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or the spell is disrupted.
**Stone Shatter** When an aeon stone swarm is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it explodes in a burst of gem shards, dealing 3d12 piercing damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Ranged** launched aeon stone +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d6 bludgeoning plus 2d6 electricity **Aeon Resonance** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) The aeon stone swarm reconfigures the aeon stones in its body, gaining a unique resonant power. This resonant power remains active until the aeon stone swarm activates a different resonant power. The aeon stone swarm can select from the following resonant powers.
  • **Defensive Resonance** The aeon stone swarm gains resistance 10 to one of the following damage types of its choice: [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697).
  • **Illumination Resonance** The aeon stone swarm glows, shedding bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color of its choice.
  • **Sensory Resonance** The aeon stone swarm gains the benefits of [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663).
  • **Subterfuge Resonance** The aeon stone swarm gains a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks, and to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to Hide and Sneak.
**Battering Orbit** ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) Each enemy in the aeon stone swarm's space takes 5d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
Aeon Stones When an aeon stone swarm is destroyed, most of the _[aeon stones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3055)_ composing its body shatter and explode, but a few remain whole. Adventurers willing to sift through mounds of sharp _aeon stone_ shards are usually rewarded with a consumed aeon stone, a _preserving aeon stone_, or a _sprouting aeon stone_—perhaps even a _nourishing aeon stone_ or _smoothing aeon stone_ for the particularly lucky!
name: Aeon Stone Swarm

<title level="1" pfs="">[Clockwork Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3892)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Clockworks are intricate, complex machines built by talented engineers and infused with magical energy. At the height of the Azlanti Empire, magitech clockworks were commonplace, from music boxes, toys, and vehicles, to bodyguards, soldiers, spies, and even nannies. 

Clockwork sentries are four-armed constructs created to serve as guardians, programmed either to prevent access to a specific location or to keep targets from escaping a secured site. This makes them the perfect clockwork for serving in prisons, dungeons, vaults, and similar locations.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Clockwork Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3892)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 83

**Perception** +16; darkvision, [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -5

**Wis** +4

**Cha** -5
</row>

[**Wind-Up**](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=223) 24 hours, DC 26, standby

**Items**
[average manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) (6)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 115

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Resistances**
physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919))

**Weaknesses**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) 10

**Sudden Seize** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature within a clockwork sentry's reach uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a space during a move action it's using; **Effect** The clockwork sentry makes an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619) the triggering creature.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pneumatic swing +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pulse blast +17 ([range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d6 force plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47)

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 23
- **1st**
[Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547)
- **3rd**
[Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677)
- **4th**
[Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675)
- **5th**
[Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751)
- **Constant (2nd)**
[See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)

**Arrest** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Trigger** The clockwork sentry has a creature [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The clockwork sentry Interacts to place manacles on the [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) creature.

**Tackle** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The clockwork sentry Strides up to twice its Speed in a straight line. If it ends this movement in reach of a creature, it makes a fist Strike against that creature. On a hit or critical hit, the target takes an additional 1d6 bludgeoning damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Clockwork Hot Spots</title>

Today, the nations of Alkenstar and New Thassilon, the church of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=333), and the famed Clockwork Cathedral of Absalom are the primary sources for clockwork creation. Haunted clockworks from the Clicking Caverns often invade the nations of Nagajor and Xa Hoi.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-223" />
name: Clockwork Sentry

markdown:
Conductors are velstracs who believe screams of anguish to be the ultimate form of musical expression. These sadistic musicians compose “joyous symphonies” from the agonized howling of their tortured, captive choirs. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Conductor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3895) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 88 **Perception** +20; greater darkvision, painsight **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +17, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 **Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions, as well as the value of those conditions. **Items** [conductor's baton](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 130 , regeneration [](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) 10 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 10 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10 **Horrifying Accompaniment** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it hears every scream the velstrac conductor's victims have ever uttered. The creature must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (frightened 4 on a critical failure).
**Sonic Redirection** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Trigger** A creature targets the velstrac conductor with an auditory or sonic effect or includes the conductor in the area of an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) effect; **Effect** The conductor sings a note that resonates with the triggering effect, redirecting it to the triggering creature. The triggering creature becomes the target of the triggering effect, instead of the conductor.
25 feet, fly 25 feet (from [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)) --- **Speed** **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d6+7 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Melee** conductor's baton +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d4+7 piercing plus 2d6 spirit **Ranged** pinpoint shriek +19 ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [range increment 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)), **Damage** 4d6 sonic **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28 - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) - **Constant (4th)** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) **Focus Gaze** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The velstrac conductor stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against the velstrac conductor's horrifying accompaniment aura. In addition, if the creature was already [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), on a failed save the creature takes 1d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Scream for Me** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** With a horrifying flourish and hungry smile, the velstrac conductor points their baton at a creature within 100 feet, compelling them to scream. The creature must attempt a DC 28 Will save. On a failure, they scream, taking 4d6 mental damage (8d6 on a critical failure) and dealing 2d6 sonic damage to all creatures in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).
Instead of targeting one creature, the conductor can choose to target all creatures in range that were compelled to scream by this ability last round. **Unholy Exaltation** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The conductor lets out a terrible scream of joy, dealing 10d6 sonic damage to all creatures in either a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) or a 90-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Holy creatures that fail this save are also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2. The conductor can't use Unholy Exaltation again for 1d4 rounds.
Instruments Conductors go to great lengths to acquire the finest instruments for their choirs, judging an individual singer's worth by their tone, emotion, endurance, and responsiveness. Vocalists who don't measure up to a conductor's standards are either played to death or passed on to other “less discerning” velstracs. Cherished instruments can expect to live a long, tortuous existence in a conductor's choir, receiving the best medical attention after each performance—at least until their voice gives out.
Tortured Friendships Velstrac conductors are fairly amiable among their kind and often work with other velstracs. They particularly get along well with [ostiarius velstracs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=848) and [velstrac evangelists](/Monsters.aspx?ID=849), who they tend to boss around. Though they find sacristans piteous, they're intrigued by their Shadow Scream ability.
name: Conductor

markdown:
Deg is an inventive engineer who is equal parts obsessed with and inspired by the Azlanti technology found throughout En-Gokal. An excellent negotiator, he's become a leader among his people, but at heart he's more concerned with the technology around him than with the well-being of his fellow deros. Luckily for Deg, these twin drives frequently align. His reign is stable and prosperous, and he's considered one of the most accomplished and innovative leaders the [deros](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=388) have ever had. **History** Deg is an older dero, around 150 years old. He's been exploring the ruins of Azlant his entire life. When he discovered the prison of En-Gokal 90 years ago, he returned to his home in the Darklands and recruited dozens of dero to follow him to the site and assist in plundering the ruin for useful magic and technology. Soon thereafter, Deg encountered Lady Nalushae. He agreed to help her and her court by providing them goods and supplies. In exchange, Deb received the freedom to explore En-Gokal, as well as the salvage rights to any unused magic and technology he and his followers discovered during their wandering. Prolonged exposure to the various vampires and their cults, as well as the ancient shrines dotted throughout En-Gokal, sparked Deg's faith in the gods collectively known as the [Children of Night](/Deities.aspx?ID=376)—[Camazotz](/Deities.aspx?ID=334), [Nin](/Deities.aspx?ID=353), [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295), [Yaezhing](/Deities.aspx?ID=318), and [Zura](/Deities.aspx?ID=443). Nizca joined Lady Nalushae at the Broken Palace three years ago and, in time, Deg and Nizca bonded over shared scientific curiosity, critical thinking, and thirst for knowledge. The pair became friends, and Deg introduced Nizca to a number of other residents of the prison, broadening the ancient vampire's horizons and giving them a spark of hope in the darkness of En-Gokal. Together, Nizca and Deg moved into the Ebon Tower, establishing their own realm in the place they hoped would be their salvation. They cleared the Ebon Tower of dangers, moved in Deg's fellow deros, and made space for Nizca's vampire followers. They freed select prisoners from stasis, using them as labor, supporters, or sources of food. Thus established, the pair began their next big challenge: transforming the quietus beam into a device capable of blotting out the sun throughout the region, thus freeing Nizca and enabling Deg and his people to live without fear of the sun. Deg named this modified device the shadow beacon, and its planning, creation, and calibration have consumed both his and Nizca's lives for the past two years. Deg was injured when the shadow beacon malfunctioned—pierced by a tether of shadow that made him something slightly more than mortal. Deg has since recovered and now considers these recent setbacks with the shadow beacon as simply part of the scientific process. Unlike Nizca, he's not averse to making mistakes, regarding his errors as opportunities to learn, innovate, and adapt. Although Deg knows the equilibrium of his home and its occupants has been affected by the arrival of Jubilant and their followers, he considers the velstracs' penchant for torture no different than his own people's fondness for experimentation, and he finds their aloof and conniving attitudes akin to that of the vampires he's worked with for nearly a century. Deg trusts that, once the shadow beacon is activated and Nizca leaves to reunite with their lost love, he will find a way to live alongside his new vicious neighbors—whether this is wishful thinking on Deg's part remains to be seen! **Campaign Role** Deg is a leader among the deros and has shaped the lives of many of the people the PCs will interact with throughout the Ebon Tower. Nizca would have neither realm nor plans for the shadow beacon were it not for Deg, and the dero's importance throughout the narrative is, in many ways, more significant than that of Nizca themself. Yet, while Nizca is driven by dreams and grand ambitions, Deg values little except the fulfillment he receives from acts of creation and discovery. As a result, over time Deg has taken on a supportive role to Nizca, essentially serving as their second in command. It might be easy for the PCs to forget that Deg is an ingenious figure in his own right, as well as a leader who has kept his community of deros safe, prosperous, and stable in an ancient Azlanti ruin overrun by [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480) and filled with volatile technology for nearly a century—an impressive accomplishment! Deg's notes should give the PCs their first and most thorough account of the history of the Ebon Tower and the modifications he and Nizca have made to the _shadow beacon_. And, of course, in the final encounter, Deg serves as Nizca's battle partner, facing off against the PCs rather than attempting to negotiate a truce, as he might have done in the past. Ultimately, Deg subscribing to Nizca's tenets of calculated mercilessness will result in his death. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Deg](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3896) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 90 **Perception** +15; darkvision **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +20, [Azlanti Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Magitech Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Items** aerosol canister, [Alchemist's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702), [greater blast boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1104), [Crafter's Eyepiece](/Equipment.aspx?ID=427), [cytillesh toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=59), [engineer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747), _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [thundering ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850) [gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=359)_, _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [ready ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2792) [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)_, [greater impact foam chassis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1115) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +21 **Will** +15 **HP** 170 **Immunities** confusion **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10, Weaknesses vulnerable to sunlight **Vulnerable to Sunlight** Deg takes 18 damage for every hour he's exposed to sunlight.
**Gas 'Em!** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Cost** 1 dose of concentrated cytillesh gas; **Trigger** A creature ends their movement adjacent to Deg; **Effect** With a flick of their wrist and a sadistic cackle, Deg triggers an aerosol canister at his hip, spraying the triggering creature with concentrated cytillesh gas. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** gauntlet +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning plus 1d6 sonic **Ranged** energy expulsor innovation +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [modular [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire sonic]](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+6 modular damage **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **3rd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **4th** [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) **Concentrated Fire** ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=606)) Deg makes two ranged Strikes with his energy expulsor innovation at a single target. Both attacks count toward his multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after he's made both attacks. **Dero Medicine** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** Deg is wearing a cytillesh toolkit and has a hand free; **Effect** Deg excises damaged flesh and crudely stitches wounds shut, healing himself or an ally in reach for 3d8+18 Hit Points. For 1 hour, the target has slashing weakness 5 and is immune to Dero Medicine. **Energy Expulsor Innovation** Deg fights using a complex device he crafted from scavenged clockwork parts and ancient Azlanti magitech. This device, called an energy expulsor innovation, is a clockwork gun bulging with multiple glass canisters, each filled with a different, roiling magical energy. The device allows Deg to use his Concentrated Fire and Overclocked Blast abilities. It otherwise functions as a magical [inventor weapon innovation](/Innovations.aspx?ID=3) with no modifications. **Overclocked Blast** Deg Interacts to set his energy expulsor innovation to the desired modular damage configuration, then overclocks his energy expulsor innovation and fires a massive blast of energy in a 100- foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). This blast deals 8d8 damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) of the type determined by the energy expulsor innovation's current configuration. Deg can't use Overclocked Blast again for 1d4 rounds. **Concentrated Cytillesh Gas** ([Uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=721), [Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528), [Inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 28; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison damage (1 round); Stage 2 5d6 poison damage and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 6d6 poison damage and stupefied 2 (1 round); Stage 4 6d6 poison damage and stupefied 3 and lose all memories made in the previous 10 minutes (1 round)

markdown:
Nizca Iricol is a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480) from the distant past, freed from stasis and fighting to escape the prison of En-Gokal. Stoic, logical, and inventive, they're a pious priest of [Nin](/Deities.aspx?ID=353) determined to perfect their body and hone their mind, reaching an enlightened state even Nin would be proud of. Yet, their fondest desire isn't related to their faith, but to their heart. Nizca seeks to reunite with their lost love, Lograsi, whom they sense is alive somewhere far to the east. This sentimental compulsion and longdormant emotion is a constant source of frustration for Nizca. To resolve this discordance, they've come up with a plan to achieves their aims while forwarding Nin's tenants—they'll fill the sky with shadows pulled from the Netherworld, enabling their escape in a way that strengthens vampires and other creatures of the night. With their competing desires justified, Nizca now pours themself into their work. **History** As a mortal in ancient Azlanti, Nizca ran a criminal empire, using their illicit proceeds to fund rabble-rousers and would-be revolutionaries. When Nizca expanded their operations and involved themself with organizations associated with undead deities like [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295) and [Zura](/Deities.aspx?ID=443), they found companionship and eventually love with Lograsi, a leading figure in a cult devoted to the velstrac demagogue Aroggus. In time, Nizca's and Lograsi's interests intertwined. Together, they accomplished much, further growing their criminal operations, undermining the Azlanti Empire, and even founding an undead cult of their own—this one devoted to the collection of deities known today as the [Children of Night](/Deities.aspx?ID=376). Recognizing a growing darkness in Nizca, Lograsi discovered a ritual that would open a pathway for a strigoi to enter their body from [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), transforming Nizca into something more than mortal. At the culmination of this ritual, Nizca became a [strigoi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=314)—an incarnation he quickly came to regard as their true self. Afterward, Nizca and Lograsi amped up their activities and eventually drew the attention of the Aeon Imperative, who raided their hideout and took Nizca into custody. Lograsi escaped and fled to Thassilon, eventually finding sanctuary in Xin-Edasseril, where they were trapped when the city became suspended in time during Earthfall. Due to the difficulties inherent in confining a strigoi, the Aeon Imperative transferred Nizca to a maximum- Nizca Iricol Calculated mastermind determined to reunite with their lover security prison in the Azlanti countryside and placed them in stasis. During this imprisonment, Earthfall struck and Azlant was destroyed. The magically fortified prison of En-Gokal remained mostly intact, though buried under rock and sea. Most of the prison's inhabitants perished. Over time, the prison's magical protections degraded, and Nizca experienced periodic moments of consciousness that felt like dreams. In these “dreams” Nizca heard words of wisdom and empowerment spoken by what they believed was the vampire deity Nin. These whispers spoke of improving oneself through deliberate thought and action. The mysterious histories of vampires and the theories of the foundations of undeath itself flowed into Nizca's mind, though such revelations would often vanish before Nizca's next period of wakefulness. Even as Nizca gained wisdom from the whispering shadows, their body shriveled and their life force ebbed. Nizca knew that when they eventually emerged from their prison, they would be a shadow of their former power. Though still locked away in stasis, Nizca grew determined not only to regain their freedom, but to perfect their vampiric form. Five years ago, a tectonic shift cracked Nizca's stasis tube, finally allowing them to awaken fully. Like many prisoners of En-Gokal before them, Nizca awoke to a destroyed world. Through the disorientation of their painful awakening, however, Nizca felt a psychic ping that Lograsi was alive and somewhere far to the east. Stranded and alone, Nizca wandered En-Gokal, eventually encountering Romi, Lady Nalushae and her court, and a community of deros led by Deg. Nizca found a true comrade in Deg, and the two embarked upon explorations together. When they discovered the quietus beam atop the Ebon Tower, they hatched a plan that would benefit both their kind: they would modify the quietus beam, tapping into the Netherworld to broadcast pure shadow into the atmosphere in an effort to block out the sun. Once the threat posed by the sun was nullified, Nizca could depart the island, and both the vampires and deros of En-Gokal could live without fear of the sun's dreaded light. **Campaign Role** Nizca is the primary antagonist of this adventure path. Their attempts to blot out the sun imperil the people of Talmandor's Bounty and to stop them, the PCs must battle their way through En-Gokal. In this book, the PCs finally encounter Nizca's forces—a collection of foes who follow Nizca for a variety of reasons. The PCs witness atrocities and liberate plenty of captives, whose stories likely drive home the horrors Nizca and their fellows are capable of committing. At the pinnacle of the Ebon Tower, in the darkness cast by the shadow beacon, the PCs finally battle Nizca and their loyal friend Deg. When the _shadow beacon_ malfunctions, the PCs must try their best to shut it down, destroy it, or reverse its hazardous effects. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Nizca Iricol](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3897) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 92 **Perception** +24; greater darkvision **Languages** [Azlanti](/Languages.aspx?ID=49), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21), [Strix](/Languages.aspx?ID=50) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Azlanti Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +22, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Magitech Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +22, [Nin Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +21, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +21 **Str** +5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 **Items** _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39)_, [greater pendant of the occult](/Equipment.aspx?ID=450), [religious symbol of Nin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745), [_scroll of chilling darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464) (5th-rank; ×2), _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ sword cane](/Weapons.aspx?ID=400)_ --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +24 **HP** 170 ([coffin restoration](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=314), [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** all physical 10 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) or weapons shedding bright light) **Weaknesses** [strigoi ](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=314) **Roiling Shades** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) 20 feet. The shadows around Nizca constantly shift and roil, battering their foes. Enemies treat the area within the aura as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).
**Calculated Escape** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** Nizca is the target of a Strike or would attempt a Reflex save against a harmful effect; **Effect** In a flash of sudden insight, Nizca calculates the trajectory of the attack, slips into shadows, and reemerges just out of the way. Nizca gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering Strike or to Reflex saves against the triggering effect. After the attack or effect is resolved, Nizca teleports to an occupied space within 20 feet; **Special** If the triggering Strike was a hit or a critical hit, or Nizca failed or critically failed the saving throw against the triggering effect, this use of Calculated Escape retroactively counts as a free action, rather than a reaction.
**Spiteful Escape** **Trigger** Nizca is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** Nizca uses Shadow Form, then flies into the shadow beacon even though they're at 0 Hit Points. While at 0 Hit Points in this form, Nizca is unaffected by further damage. Once they reach the shadow beacon, their spirit becomes permanently fused with it and is destroyed only when the shadow beacon is destroyed. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet (on manifested batlike wings of shadow) **Melee** sword cane +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Concealable](/Traits.aspx?ID=560), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d10+11 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **1st** [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Share Lore](/Spells.aspx?ID=2363) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) - **4th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [Telepathy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719) - **5th** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) (×2) - **6th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×5), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) **Cleric Domain Spells 3 Focus Points,** DC 30, attack +22 - **6th** [Perfected Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1831), [Perfected Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1830), [Touch of Undeath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1846) **Channel Smite** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Nizca siphons death through a melee attack and into their foe. Nizca expends a [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) spell and makes a melee Strike. On a hit, they cast the 1-action version of the expended spell to damage the target, in addition to the normal damage from the Strike. The target automatically gets a failure on its save (or a critical failure if Nizca's Strike was a critical hit). The spell doesn't have the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait when cast this way. **Drink Essence** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or willing creature is within Nizca's reach; **Effect** As [strigoi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=314). When Drinking Essence, Nizca regains 15 Hit Points. **Reach Spell** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) If the next action Nizca uses is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet. **Shadow Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) As [strigoi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=314).

markdown:
While choral angels are content to sing their hymns in [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44), they must sometimes take to the mortal realm to provide needed succor for those engaged in righteous battle against the wicked. An angelic chorus often acts as support for armies who have gained the favor of the celestial realms. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Angelic Chorus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3898) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 172 **Perception** +22; darkvision **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +25, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +24 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +22 **Will** +25 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 210 (4 segments) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 15 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10, [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 10 **Harmonizing Aura** ([ <%TRAITS%542%%> aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%579%%> divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) <%END>, [ <%TRAITS%697%%> sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765) <%END>) 20 feet. Allies in the aura gain a +2 status bonus to sonic damage rolls and a +1 status bonus to AC and all saves against effects with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) trait. Enemies in the aura take a –2 status penalty to sonic damage rolls and a –1 status penalty to AC and all saves against auditory and sonic effects.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 19, attack +24 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Courageous Anthem](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763), [Uplifting Overture](/Spells.aspx?ID=1779) - **3rd** [Counter Performance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1762) (at will) - **4th** [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) (at will), [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469) (at will), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616) (at will) - **5th** [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616) - **6th** [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [Zealous Conviction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1760) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 29 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) **Admonishing Hymn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The angelic choir raises their voices in song, singing a note that vibrates the very fabric of being. This takes the form of a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 90 feet that deals 6d6 sonic damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). A creature who critically fails the save is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 minute. When the angelic chorus is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 10-foot burst. **Harmonized Spellcasting** When the angelic chorus Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that deals sonic damage, that spell gains a status bonus to damage equal to its rank. **Righteous Clamor** ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** With sonorous shouts, the angelic chorus lashes out with their fists in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation (DC 29 basic Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8 bludgeoning damage plus 1d6 sonic damage
2d8+7 bludgeoning damage plus 2d6 sonic damage
3d8+10 bludgeoning damage plus 2d6 sonic damage
name: Angelic Chorus

Animated statues usually appear in pairs or alone, hiding in plain sight among the other decor. Occasionally, a creator will fashion dozens of animated statues to serve as a nighunstoppable fighting force that doesn't complain or tire. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Animated Army](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3899) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 173 **Perception** +13; darkvision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +0 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 27 (23 when broken) **Fort** +16 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 construct armor **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Hardness** 10 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 **Construct Armor** Like normal objects, the animated statues of the animated army have Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage the animated army takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an animated army is reduced to less than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks, removing the Hardness and reducing its Armor Class to 23.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Battering Fists** ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The animated army makes a melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 bludgeoning damage
2d8+8 bludgeoning damage
3d8+10 bludgeoning damage
name: Animated Army

Students at academies that teach magic sometimes form fast friendships. When not studying or blowing off steam together, these cliques might be called to defend their place of learning from outside attack. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Apprentice Magician Clique](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3900) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 173 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +12 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +5 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +12 **Will** +15 **HP** 75 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 4, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 4 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 22, attack +15 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Dizzying Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500), [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) - **2nd** [Blazing Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) - **3rd** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753) **Barrage of Force** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) The apprentice magicians launch shards of pure magic at all creatures in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet. This barrage deals 5d4 force damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the clique is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Clique Spellcasting** When the apprentice magician clique Casts a Spell, its members pool their arcane power into the spell. A creature who critically fails their save against the spell or whom the clique hits with a critical spell attack is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute. **Sparking Wands** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The apprentice magicians wave wands that shoot out minor bolts of electricity at short range. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d8 electricity damage
2d8+3 electricity damage
2d8+7 electricity damage
name: Apprentice Magician Clique

markdown:
Though arboreal wardens typically remain within the boundaries of the forests they protect, they occasionally gather into copses to seek information about potential threats to report back to arboreal regents. Arboreals are typically slow to act, but copses must sometimes make quick judgment calls when faced with imminent dangers. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Arboreal Copse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3901) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 173 **Perception** +18; low-light vision **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 27 (29 with shield raised) **Fort** +20 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 **HP** 150 (4 segments) **Resistances** bludgeoning 8, piercing 8 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, axes 8, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 12, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Reactive Attack** **Trigger** An enemy within 5 feet of the arboreal copse uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The arboreals swing their stone swords. The triggering enemy takes 2d8+9 bludgeoning damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the creature critically fails their saving throw and the trigger was a manipulate action, the troop disrupts that action.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Raise Shields** The arboreal wardens of the copse raise their shields in tandem, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and Reflex saves. **Shoving Shield Wall** The arboreal copse Strides. All enemies whose square the copse begins in or passes through during their movement take 5d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude). On a failed saving throw, the arboreal copse carries the creature along on their shields, moving them in the same distance and direction for the rest of their Stride. **Sword Bash** The arboreal copse uses their blunt stone longswords to pummel its foes. Each enemy in a 10- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+1 bludgeoning damage
2d8+9 bludgeoning damage
3d8+10 bludgeoning damage
name: Arboreal Copse

markdown:
Archer regiments are capable of filling the sky with arrows at great distances, making them vital to any war leader who wishes to weaken the enemy before they get close to allied units or encampments. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Archer Regiment](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3902) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 174 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +22 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +25 **Will** +22 **HP** 210 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Dagger Defense** The archer regiment draws daggers to attack close-range enemies. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions. The archer regiment gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the beginning of their next turn.
2d4+2 piercing damage
4d4+12 piercing damage
4d4+15 piercing damage **Drilled in Formations** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The archer regiment uses [Change Formation](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=543). An archer regiment unit typically knows the loose and marching column formations. **Rain of Arrows** The archer regiment fires their longbows in a coordinated volley. This volley is either a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 200 feet that deals 4d8 piercing damage or a 10- foot burst within 100 feet that deals 6d8 piercing damage. Either effect has a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. When the archer regiment is reduced to 2 segments, both areas are reduced by 5 feet.
name: Archer Regiment

Rekheps, also known as [shield archons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=33), stand against any fiendish onslaughts on [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), but occasionally travel to the mortal Universe to protect the weak. Large formations of rekheps are a sight to behold as they lock their shield forms in place to face the wicked. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Archon Bastion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3903) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 174 **Perception** +30; darkvision **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +32, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +28, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +26 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +9 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 37 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +30 **Ref** +25 **Will** +28 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 300 (4 segments) **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15, [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 15 **Archon's Aegis** **Trigger** An enemy damages an ally of the archon bastion and both are within 15 feet of the archon bastion; **Effect** The ally gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 20 to all damage against the triggering damage and the enemy takes 1d8+4 piercing damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **2nd** [Share Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669) (at will) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Fearless Switch** ([Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The archon bastion Strides so that at least one of its segments occupies the same space of a Large or smaller willing ally. That willing ally is then teleported to any open space it can fit into that is adjacent to any of the archon bastion's segments, using teleportation magic innate to the troop's individual shield archons. The archon bastion can move up to three allies in this fashion. **Living Shields** The archon bastion grants each ally within a 5-foot emanation a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until that ally is no longer within the area or until the start of the archon bastion's next turn, whichever comes first. If the archon bastion uses Archon's Aegis against an attack against one of the shielded allies, the archon bastion gains the resistance and takes the damage rather than the ally. **Smiting Lances** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The archon bastion engages in a uniform melee attack against each enemy in 10-foot emanation (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions. An [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creature that fails its save against this effect takes an additional 2d6 spirit damage (or 1d6 spirit damage for the one-action version).
1d8+4 piercing damage
[two-actions] 3d8+14 piercing damage
[three-actions] 4d8+19 piercing damage
name: Archon Bastion

The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
2d6 bludgeoning damage
3d6+10 bludgeoning damage
4d6+13 bludgeoning damage **Chorus of Croaks** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The boggard dreadknot unleashes a chorus of terrifying croaks. Any non-boggard within 30 feet becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 unless they succeed at a DC 26 Will save; those who critically succeed are temporarily immune for 1 minute. **Javelin Barrage** The dreadknot draws javelins and launches a coordinated barrage. This barrage is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet that deals 4d6 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the dreadknot is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Mounted Troop** **Effects** that target only animals or only humanoids may not work on the boggard dreadknot, subject to the GM's discretion. **Swamp Passage** A boggard dreadknot ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by swamp terrain features. **Tongue Lashing** The dreadknot's giant frogs lash out at each enemy in a 15-foot emanation with their tongues, requiring them to succeed at a DC 26 Reflex save or become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). A creature grabbed in this way isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), but it can't move more than 15 feet from the dreadknot. A creature can sever one of the tongues with a Strike against AC 25 that deals at least 15 slashing damage. This doesn't deal damage to the dreadknot.
name: Boggard Dreadknot

[Charau-ka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1533) are small humanoid apes who live in tropical jungles, especially within the Mwangi Expanse. Shrieker crews patrol the canopies close to charau-ka settlements, keeping predators at bay. Sometimes, a group of charauka will devote themselves to the demon lord Angazhan and become a bloodthirsty hunting party. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Charau-Ka Shrieker Crew](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3906) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 175 **Perception** +16; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Mwangi](/Languages.aspx?ID=39) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +19 **Will** +15 **HP** 135 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Frenzied Hatchets** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The charau-ka engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+2 slashing damage
2d6+10 slashing damage
3d6+13 slashing damage **Shrieking Charge** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) With raucous shrieks, the shrieker crew charges forward. The troop [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice. If they move at least 10 feet, the crew deals 3d6+13 slashing damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to each enemy in a 5-foot emanation at the end of the crew's movement. Each target who fails this save is also deafened for 1 round. **Storm of Daggers** The charau-ka hurl daggers as a ranged attack in the form of a barrage. This barrage is a 10-foot burst within 30 feet that deals 5d4 piercing damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The range increases by 15 feet if the charau-ka are above their targets. When the shrieker crew is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst.
name: Charau-Ka Shrieker Crew

markdown:
With each unit being 500 pounds of metal and magic, a clockwork infantry is a force to be reckoned with on the battlefield. Needing no food, shelter, or rest and feeling no pain, clockwork infantry can advance where living units have great trouble. With no morale that can be broken or minds that can be beguiled, they patrol without stopping and show no mercy. The only drawback is the need to wind them more often than simpler clockwork mechanics. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Clockwork Infantry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3907) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 176 **Perception** +21; darkvision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -5 [**Wind-Up**](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=223) 24 hours, DC 27, standby
 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 **HP** 195 (4 segments) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 8 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, splash damage 10, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) 10 **Reactive Sweep** **Trigger** An enemy within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square in the area during a move action it's using; **Effect** The clockwork infantry lashes out with their halberds. The triggering enemy takes 2d10+10 damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the enemy critically fails this saving throw and the trigger was a manipulate action, the damage disrupts that action.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Halberd Sweep** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The clockwork infantry engages in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d10+2 piercing or slashing damage
2d10+10 piercing or slashing damage
3d10+12 piercing or slashing damage **Raise Defenses** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The clockwork soldiers of the infantry extend external plates on mechanical actuators to defend the troop or an adjacent creature. The creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of the infantry's next turn, or until it is no longer adjacent to the infantry, whichever comes first.
name: Clockwork Infantry

Clockwork runners are units build for speed. Mimicking large cats, they sacrifice sturdiness for agility. Runners provide excellent battlefield control by speedily traversing diverse terrain types, able to cut off enemy units and provide covering fire for allies with their built-in crossbows. As clockworks, they do need to be rewound, making them less ideal for longer deployments. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Clockwork Runner Pack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3908) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 176 **Perception** +12; darkvision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +0 **Int** -5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -5 [Wind-Up](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=223) 48 hours, DC 19, standby
 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +15 **Will** +9 **HP** 75 (4 segments) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), nonlethal attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2919) 5 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Fire Crossbows** The clockwork runners reload the crossbows built onto their backs, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet that deals 2d8 piercing damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the clockwork runners are reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Scratch and Bite** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The clockwork runners engage in a pack attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8 piercing or slashing damage
2d8+3 piercing or slashing damage
2d8+7 piercing or slashing damage **War Pounce** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The clockwork runner pack Strides, [ignoring difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) (but not [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586)). At the end of this movement, each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 1d8 piercing or slashing damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
name: Clockwork Runner Pack

Deinonychuses are widely known and feared both for the razor-sharp claws with which they savagely disembowel their prey and for their unmatched mastery of pack tactics, which enables determined deinonychus packs to consistently bring down prey much larger than themselves. Some iruxi and [xulgath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=486) communities train these dinosaurs to act as battlefield skirmishers, weakening their enemies with hit-and-run tactics before closing in for the kill. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Deinonychus Pack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3909) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 175 **Perception** +15; low-light vision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 6, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 6 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Jaws and Claws** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The pack makes a melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 slashing or piercing damage plus 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)
2d6+4 slashing or piercing damage plus 2d4 persistent bleed damage
3d6+6 slashing or piercing damage plus 2d4 persistent bleed damage **Predator's Advantage** Bleeding creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to Reflex saves against a deinonychus pack's Jaws and Claws. **Surround Prey** **Requirements** The deinonychus pack has at least 3 segments; **Effect** The pack Strides, positioning its segments so that at least two of them are adjacent to the same creature, and lashes out with its talons. That creature must succeed at a DC 22 Reflex save or take 2d4 persistent bleed damage
name: Deinonychus Pack

markdown:
It is thankfully uncommon to encounter more than one [dezullon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2912) within a few square miles, largely because few ecosystems contain enough prey to sustain more than one of the ravenous flesh-eating plants for long. When a well-fed dezullon finds a suitably abundant location, however, it occasionally takes root, blooms, and shortly thereafter expels dozens of fist-sized seed pods. Should enough of the resulting saplings survive to full size, they temporarily merge into a tangled mass of grasping tendrils and sweep across the area, voraciously scouring their environment clean of any remaining game before dispersing to seek their own hunting grounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Dezullon Thicket](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3910) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 177 **Perception** +25; low-light vision **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +29 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +28 **Will** +23 **HP** 270 (4 segments) , regeneration [](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) 30 (deactivated by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 20 **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **Regrowth** When the dezullon thicket's regeneration raises its Hit Points above a listed threshold after losing a segment for dropping below it, the thicket immediately regains that lost segment.
**[Stench](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=76)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 30 feet, DC 33
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Acid Rain** The dezullon thicket discharges a cascade of acidic digestive juices as a ranged attack, dealing 6d6 acid damage in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) and exposing any creature struck to amnesia venom. When the thicket is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Amnesia Venom** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 33 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); Stage 2 clumsy 2 (1 round); Stage 3 [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and clumsy 3 (1 round); Stage 4 as Stage 3 and permanently forget the last hour (1 round) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d10+6 bludgeoning, DC 36 **Mass Improved Grab** **Trigger** A creature fails or critically fails their Reflex save against the dezullon thicket's Thrashing Vines; **Effect** The dezullon thicket attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the triggering creature. A dezullon thicket can Grapple as many creatures as it has remaining segments, though it needs to spend an action to extend the duration on subsequent rounds. These attempts neither apply nor count toward the creature's multiple attack penalty. **Root** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until the next time it acts, the dezullon thicket appears to be a field of normal pitcher plants. It has an automatic result of 49 (53 in forests or swamps) on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as a grove of noncreature plants. **Thrashing Vines** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The thicket makes a melee attack against each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+3 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid
2d8+11 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid and Mass [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66)
3d8+12 bludgeoning plus 3d6 acid and Mass Improved Grab
name: Dezullon Thicket
