Though drakes from different biomes rarely interact with one another due to geographical distance, a powerful creature, ancient artifact, or natural catastrophe could bring together multiple types of drakes into a single terrifying, living catastrophe. Such a flight can be a terror to behold on the battlefield. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Drake Flight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3911) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 178 **Perception** +23; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 **HP** 240 (4 segments) **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 5, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Tail Lashes** **Trigger** A creature within 10 feet of the drake flight uses an action to Strike or attempt a skill check; **Effect** The drakes lash out with their tails, dealing 3d8+10 bludgeoning damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the triggering creature fails the save, they also take a –2 circumstance penalty to the triggering roll.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 50 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Draconic Onslaught** The drakes frenzy, lashing out with fangs and tails. Each enemy in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) attempts a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d8 piercing or bludgeoning damage
3d8+10 piercing or bludgeoning damage
4d8+14 piercing or bludgeoning damage **Drake Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Certain drakes within the flight bring their breath weapon (or similar ability) to bear, exhaling energy that explodes in a 15-foot burst within 120 feet. This explosion deals 5d6 acid, cold, fire, or poison damage; the ability gains the corresponding trait. The drake flight can't choose the same damage type until it uses this ability with a different damage type. When the drakes are reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 10-foot burst. **Speed Surge** **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The drake flight Strides or Flies twice.
name: Drake Flight

Numerous [dromaar](/Traits.aspx?ID=516) mercenary companies operate in and around Belkzen, providing their strength at arms to any hold that requests their services. These disciplined warriors march confidently into battle, bringing their axes to bear against all who stand in their way. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Dromaar Company](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3912) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 178 **Perception** +14; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +11 **HP** 90 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **Ferocious Fall** **Trigger** The dromaar company is about to lose a segment due to passing a Hit Point threshold; **Effect** The dying dromaar mercenaries lash out as they fall. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 1d8 slashing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save); this occurs before the troop loses a segment.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Bola Hurl** The dromaars draw bolas and toss them in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature in this area takes 3d6 nonlethal bludgeoning damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who fails this saving throw is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Charge the Fallen** The dromaar company Strides up to twice, sweeping with their axes. They deal 2d8+5 slashing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) at the end of their movement. This damage increases by 5 if the target is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Disciplined Strikes** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dromaars coordinate melee attacks with their axes against all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8 slashing damage
2d8+5 slashing damage
2d8+10 slashing damage
name: Dromaar Company

In their role as protectors of the wild and unspoiled areas of the world, powerful [druids](/Traits.aspx?ID=583) might gather together to stand against a particularly heinous threat. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Druid Circle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3913) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 179 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +25 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +22 **Will** +25 **HP** 210 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 32, attack +26 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Caustic Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) - **4th** [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) - **6th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714) **Call Down the Storm** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) The druids summon wind and lightning against all creatures in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 80 feet. This storm deals 3d10 electricity damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who fails the save is also pushed 5 feet away from the druid circle. When the druid circle is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Sickle and Staff** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The druids strike out in a coordinated melee attack against all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
2d6 bludgeoning or slashing damage
4d6+9 bludgeoning or slashing damage
5d6+13 bludgeoning or slashing damage **Troop Spellcasting** When the druid circle [Casts a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734), its constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), or [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
name: Druid Circle

markdown:
While many dwarves train to master hammers and shields, others seek to become masters of ranged weapons, usually sturdy and dwarven-built crossbows. Dwarven armies employ longshot squads as the first line of defense, especially outside of cramped tunnels and subterranean caverns. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Dwarf Longshot Squad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3914) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 179 **Perception** +22; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22 **Str** +1 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +22 **Will** +16 **HP** 180 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 12, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 12 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Frequency** once per round **Effect** Using blades attached to their crossbows, the dwarven longshots engages in a coordinated melee attack against enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 piercing damage
2d8+11 piercing damage
3d8+13 piercing damage **Hampering Fusillade** The dwarven longshots fire dozens of bolts in quick succession to slow down advancing enemies. Each creature in a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet must attempt a DC 26 Fortitude saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed for 1 minute. Spending an Interact action to remove the bolts ends this penalty. **Bolts from the Blue** The dwarven longshots reload their crossbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet that deals 6d6 piercing damage with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex saving throw. When the dwarven longshot squad is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 10-foot burst.
Longshots With Guns Dwarves from Dongun Hold and similar places have access to firearms, and you might choose to reflect this by giving the dwarf longshot squad rifles. Adjust the stat block to have the uncommon trait and replace mentions of “[crossbows](/Weapons.aspx?ID=425)” to “rifles” and “bolts” to “bullets.” In addition, give the Bolts from the Blue action the concussive trait, meaning that it deals bludgeoning or piercing damage, whichever would be more detrimental to each target (you may also want to refer to it as “Bullets from the Blue”). Finally, replace Hampering Fusillade with the following ability. **Bullet Smog** The dwarven longshots fire their rifles in rapid succession to create a cloud of smoke within 120 feet. This cloud is a 20-foot burst and lasts for 1 minute or until it is dispersed by a strong wind, whichever comes first. All creatures within the cloud become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it.
name: Dwarf Longshot Squad

Soldiers who participate in multiple campaigns of war gain the experience necessary to become their military's finest fighting forces. First-class infantry troops are deployed on battlefields where winning the day is of the utmost importance. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [First-Class Infantry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3915) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 180 **Perception** +23 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 **HP** 240 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 12, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 12 **No Retreat** These soldiers have been extensively trained to hold their ground no matter the situation. If any effect would force the first-class infantry to move, reduce the distance by 10 feet. Any time they would be affected by the fleeing condition, the first-class infantry is instead [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 2 for the same duration.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Bolt Salvo** The first-class infantry draws, loads, and shoots a salvo from their crossbows. The salvo is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet that deals 4d8 piercing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the first-class infantry is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Clash of Steel** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The first-class infantry attacks each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d6+1 slashing damage
4d6+10 slashing damage
4d6+18 slashing damage **Drilled in Formations** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The first-class infantry uses [Change Formation](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=543). An first-class infantry unit typically knows the marching column and wedge formations. **First-class Charge** The first-class infantry rushes forward with a hunger for battle. They [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice their Speed. At the end of their movement, each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 2d6+5 slashing damage, with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.
name: First-Class Infantry

markdown:
While fleshwarpers would like every creation to be perfect, they produce countless rejects. Many of these are killed at creation. But some still have some use, turning imperfection into some kind of weapon. These rejects— mounds of flesh, carapaces, and too many limbs—can be whipped into a frenzy and set against enemy forces. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Fleshwarp Amalgam](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3916) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 180 **Perception** +16; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); can't speak any language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 135 (4 segments) **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Brutal Retaliation** **Trigger** The fleshwarp amalgam loses a segment due to passing a Hit Point threshold; **Effect** The fleshwarp amalgam lashes out in retaliation. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 2d10+6 bludgeoning or slashing damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex). A creature who fails the save is also pushed 5 feet away from the amalgam.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Acid Spray** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)) The fleshwarp amalgam sprays acid from their various orifices, combining the streams into a powerful spray. This acid spray is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) that deals 3d8 acid damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) within 60 feet. A creature who critically fails their saving throw takes 1d8 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). When the troop is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Frenzy of Tentacles and Claws** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The fleshwarps make wild melee attacks against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 23 basic Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d10 bludgeoning or slashing damage
2d10+6 bludgeoning or slashing damage
2d10+11 bludgeoning or slashing damage **Many-Limbed Stride** While moving on land, the fleshwarp amalgam ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain.
name: Fleshwarp Amalgam

markdown:
Normally solitary beasts that lurk in abandoned temples and other structures that afford them a place to hide among appropriately monstrous statuary, gargoyles occasionally band together to hunt challenging prey or simply to take communal pleasure in slaughtering the defenseless. Called wings, these groupings are usually short-lived, though that matters little to those who fall victim to their predations in the meantime. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Gargoyle Wing](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3917) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 181 **Perception** +18; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +17 **Will** +17 **HP** 150 (4 segments) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915)) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Death From Above** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Trigger** The gargoyle wing is Flying, and a creature moves into an adjacent square below it; **Effect** The gargoyle wing swoops down with their talons. The triggering creature takes 2d8+9 slashing damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Catch and Release** The gargoyle wing attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), comparing the result to the Fortitude DC of a number of Large or smaller creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) equal to the gargoyle wing's remaining number of segments, then Flies up to 40 feet, bringing any successfully [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) creatures along, and Releases them. **Raking Swoop** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The gargoyle wing rips and tears with their stony talons at each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 slashing damage
2d8+9 slashing damage
3d8+11 slashing damage
name: Gargoyle Wing

markdown:
Like their tiny counterparts, colonies of giant ants often form vast armies that launch regular campaigns into the lands surrounding their nests to forage for food and expand their territory. Though their primary targets are usually rival colonies, a giant ant army that happens upon a humanoid settlement will not hesitate to overrun and strip it of resources to bring back to their queen, taking a particular interest in the settlement's former inhabitants. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Giant Ant Army](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3918) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 181 **Perception** +15; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -4 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +12 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 7, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 7 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Giant Ant Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d8 poison damage and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d10 poison damage and enfeebled 3 (1 round) **Grasping Mandibles** **Requirements** The giant ant army's last action was a Mandible Frenzy that at least one creature failed their save against or the giant ant army has at least one creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** If used after Grasping Mandibles, the giant ant army can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), comparing the result to the Fortitude DC of each creature who failed its saving throw, up to as many creatures as the giant ant army has remaining segments. The giant any army can instead use Grasping Mandibles to choose one creature it's grabbing or restraining to automatically extend that condition to the end of the army's next turn. **Haul Away** **Requirements** The giant ant army has at least one creature [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The army Strides up to its Speed, carrying any restrained creatures with it. If the creature is Gargantuan, the giant ant army is [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70). **Mandible Frenzy** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The army makes a savage bite attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d8 slashing damage plus Grasping Mandibles
2d8+6 slashing damage plus Grasping Mandibles
2d8+11 slashing damage **Overwhelm** The giant ant army swarms over a Large or larger creature that it has grabbed, pinning the creature in place and causing it to become [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) until the start of the giant ant army's next turn or until it Escapes. A creature that begins its turn restrained by the army is repeatedly stung by the clinging ants, automatically taking 2d6 piercing damage and suffering the effects of giant ant venom.
name: Giant Ant Army

markdown:
Inventive gnomes blend engineering and fey magic to create wondrous cannons, whose colorful blasts dazzle foes while creating areas of warped terrain. Only the cleverest generals understand how to use cannon corps to great advantage in unconventional warfare. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Gnome Cannon Corps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3919) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 182 **Perception** +15; low-light vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +15 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +6 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Arcane Explosion** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) Aiming the gnome cannons toward the enemy, loading them, and firing them requires the troop's full attention. The cannons fire a 15- foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) of bright magic within 200 feet that deals 2d12+2 force damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails their save is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round; this is a [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect. The area of the explosion seems to twist and ripple for 1 minute afterward. A creature that attempts to move through the space must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or treat the area as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366); this is an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) and visual effect. **Cannon Vent** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The gnome engineers vent the cannons' energy in a blast that hits all creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+2 fire damage
2d6+8 fire damage
3d6+10 fire damage **Direct Hit** The gnomes fire a more mundane round from one of their cannons at a single target within 60 feet, who takes 3d10+6 bludgeoning damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a failed save, the creature is also pushed 5 feet away from the troop.
name: Gnome Cannon Corps

markdown:
[Goblins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=422) lack the organization and discipline of their hobgoblin cousins but often more than make up for it with guile. Some goblin tribes like to terrorize trade routes under the cover of dusk. Focused on stripping valuables and escaping rather than finishing a fight, these groups of goblins excel at speed and identifying things of value to strip from their bewildered prey, using hit-and-run tactics since they tend to be outmatched more often than not. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Goblin Rabble](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3920) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 182 **Perception** +11; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +12 **Str** +1 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +14 **Will** +8 **HP** 60 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 4, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 4 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Dogpile** The goblin rabble engage in as coordinated an attack as they can with their dogslicers, attacking each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions. A creature who critically fails their save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
1d6 slashing damage
2d6+4 slashing damage
2d6+7 slashing damage **Hobble Pursuit** The goblin rabble hamstring and hobble as many enemies as possible. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds.
**Failure** The creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds and is slowed 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds and is slowed 1. **Rush and Steal** Quickly moving in with grasping hands, the goblin rabble take what they can. The goblin rabble [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice their Speed. During this movement, the goblins Interact to pick up an unattended object no larger than 2 Bulk or attempt to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) an item from a creature they are adjacent to; the goblins can pick up or Steal as many objects as they have remaining segments in any combination.
name: Goblin Rabble

markdown:
Some [halflings](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=426), especially those from Varisia, study the methods of Harrow fortune-telling and tap into occult forces that let them manipulate fate using the cards. Some mercenary companies and criminal organizations employ such halflings as bad-luck charms for their enemies. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Halfling Lucky Draw](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3921) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 182 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +16, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 **HP** 135 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 23, attack +20 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **3rd** [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) (3 slots) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) (2 slots) **Bad Deal** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The halflings mock and taunt their enemies with quick Harrow readings that predict doom. The troop chooses a number of creatures equal to the number of its remaining segments within 60 feet. Each target must attempt a DC 23 Will save. On a failure, the target must roll their next attack roll, saving throw, or skill check twice and use the worse result. **False Cuts** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The halflings feint with their cards and then lash out with their daggers in a coordinated melee attack against enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d4 piercing or slashing damage and 1d4 precision damage
2d4+7 piercing or slashing damage and 2d4 precision damage
3d4+10 piercing or slashing damage and 2d4 precision damage **Troop Harrowing** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When the halfling lucky draw Casts a Spell that targets a single creature, some of the constituent members can perform a focused Harrow reading on the target as part of Casting the Spell. The lucky draw attempts an [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) skill check against the target's Will DC. On a success, the target takes a –1 status penalty to their saving throw or AC against the spell (–2 on a critical success). If the lucky draw critically fails this check, their reading portends bad news for the halflings and they become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2.
name: Halfling Lucky Draw

markdown:
Frequently deployed on the battlefield alongside [vordine legions](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3941), hell hound packs distract and weaken enemy forces to provide their masters with a tactical advantage. While not intelligent enough to employ advanced maneuvers, well-trained hell hounds are adept at using their flaming breath to control the battlefield by separating enemy units from their allies or forcing them into less favorable positions, leaving them easy prey for other fiendish troops. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Hell Hound Pack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3922) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 183 **Perception** +16; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117); can't speak any language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +17 **Will** +14 **HP** 135 (4 segments) **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 **Hellish Revenge** **Trigger** The hell hound pack is critically hit by a Strike or spell attack; **Effect** The hell hound pack's Hellfire Breath recharges. They can immediately use it as part of this reaction.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Hellfire Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The hell hounds in the pack combine their efforts to bathe the battlefield in hellish flame, dealing 2d10 fire damage to all creatures in two 15- foot [cones](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) that can't overlap. The hell hound pack can't use Hellfire Breath again for 1d4 rounds. If the pack would take [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage or be targeted by a fire effect, its Hellfire Breath recharges. **Infernal Mauling** ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The pack tears into each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with their flaming jaws (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d4 piercing damage plus 1d6 fire damage
1d8+7 piercing damage plus 2d6 fire damage
2d8+7 piercing damage plus 2d6 fire damage

markdown:
There are few sights as intimidating as a regiment of wellarmed [hobgoblin](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=430) soldiers advancing across a battlefield. Exceptionally capable of defending themselves and tenacious to a fault, such hobgoblins are used as a precision tool, exploiting the enemy's weakest point and tearing it wide open to collapse entire defensive lines. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Hobgoblin Veteran Regiment](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3923) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 183 **Perception** +18; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +15 **Will** +19 **HP** 150 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Hobgoblin Phalanx** Many of the hobgoblins raise their shields to protect their allies. The regiment gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)
**Watchful** The hobgoblin regiment is trained to guard from all sides with shields at the ready. The hobgoblin regiment can't be made [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) by [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) creatures of the troop's level or lower. --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Disciplined Strikes** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The hobgoblins engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 slashing damage
2d8+9 slashing damage
3d8+11 slashing damage **Overrun** The hobgoblin veteran regiment marches inexorably forward, crushing enemies in their path. The regiment Strides up to double its Speed and can move through the spaces of Large or smaller creatures, dealing 2d8+9 slashing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to each creature whose space it enters. The regiment can attempt to Overrun the same creature only once in a single use of Overrun. When the regiment is reduced to 2 segments, the damage decreases to 1d8+2 slashing damage. **Shortbow Volley** The hobgoblins draw their shortbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet that deals 4d6 piercing damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. When the regiment is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385).
name: Hobgoblin Veteran Regiment

[Kobold](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=435) trap squads generally protect their warrens, but these groups sometimes range outside of their lairs at the behest of a powerful patron. Their goals are usually to scout a given location, but they can be tasked with procuring an item or resource for the good of the warren. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Kobold Trap Squad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3924) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 184 **Perception** +11; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Group Scamper** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The kobolds Stride up to their Speed plus 5 feet and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against reactions triggered by this movement. If they end this movement with at least 1 segment adjacent to any enemy, the squad is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of its next turn. **Hasty Traps** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The kobolds hastily prepare a handful of rudimentary traps in their vicinity until the beginning of their next turn. The next creature who moves adjacent to the trap squad triggers a trap and must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d4 persistent bleed damage (2d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) on a critical failure). A creature taking persistent bleed damage from Hasty Traps takes a –5-foot enhancement penalty to its Speed. This occurs to as many creatures as the kobold trap squad has segments when it performed the action, but a single creature can trigger only one trap per turn. **Sling Barrage** The kobolds draw their slings, then launch a ranged barrage of stones. This barrage is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 50 feet that deals 3d4 bludgeoning damage with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. When the squad is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Spear Jabs** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The kobold trap squad engages in a coordinated melee attack against all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 piercing damage
2d6+4 piercing damage
2d6+7 piercing damage
name: Kobold Trap Squad

[Daemons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=386) are shaped by, and devoted to, the destruction of life in all its forms. [Leukodaemons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2893) serve the Apocalypse Rider of Pestilence, spreading disease across the Universe with their very touch. A small army of leukodaemons has the potential to wipe out a small country—or more—if left unchecked. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Leukodaemon Plague](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3925) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 184 **Perception** +25; darkvision, plaguesense (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +25, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Plaguesense** A leukodaemon plague senses any creature with a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), and knows the type and current stage of all diseases carried by any creature within range. --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +28 **HP** 255 (4 segments) **Immunities** [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **Infectious Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) 30 feet. Leukodaemons radiate infection. All creatures within 30 feet of a leukodaemon plague take a –2 status penalty to saves against disease. If a creature within range contracts or progresses a disease, all adjacent creatures are exposed to the same disease, at the same DC.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34 - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) **Daemonic Pestilence** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) The leukodaemon plague can telepathically communicate with the afflicted creature at any distance on the same plane; **Saving Throw** DC 34 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 5** drained 3 (1 week); Stage 6 dead **Infected Jaws and Claws** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The leukodaemons unleash an onslaught of blows against each enemy in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d10+3 piercing or slashing damage plus daemonic pestilence
3d10+9 piercing or slashing damage plus daemonic pestilence
4d10+12 piercing or slashing damage plus daemonic pestilence **Pestilent Wheeze** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The leukodaemons exhale a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of disease-ridden black flies that deal 5d8 piercing damage (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails the save also becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (or sickened 2 on a critical failure). When the leukodaemon plague is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 20-foot cone. **Quicken Pestilence** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The leukodaemons coax a disease into full bloom. They choose a target within their infectious aura that's currently affected by a disease. That creature must attempt a Fortitude save against the disease as if the interval for the disease's current stage had passed.
name: Leukodaemon Plague

The process of turning oneself into a [lich](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=438) is rare and rigorous, but in places where magic flows freely, a society of spellcasters might perform the rituals as a collective. When successful, the resultant liches are spiritually bound together, forming an army of dangerous undead spellcasters, often with sinister goals. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Lich Legion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3926) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 185 **Perception** +30; darkvision **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +29 **Str** +1 **Dex** +5 **Con** +0 **Int** +9 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +30 **Will** +33 +1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **HP** 330 (4 segments, mass rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, physical 15 (except magical bludgeoning) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 37
**Mass Rejuvenation** This functions similarly to a [lich's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=438) rejuvenation ability, though with all the liches of a legion returning as a troop thanks to a collective soul cage, which is a level 18 item that has Hardness 15 and 54 Hit Points.
**Troop Counterspell** **Trigger** A creature within the legion's sight casts a spell the legion has prepared; **Effect** The lich legion expends a prepared spell to [counter](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the triggering creature's casting of that same spell. The lich legion loses the spell slot as if they had cast the triggering spell. The lich legion then attempts to counteract the triggering spell with a +2 status bonus to the counteract check.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 40, attack +35 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) (×2), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [False Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Fire Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1529), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **5th** [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) (×2), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1750) - **6th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462) (×2), [Never Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508) (×2), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) - **8th** [Arctic Rift](/Spells.aspx?ID=1444) (×2), [Desiccate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1484), [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507) - **9th** [Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521), [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596), [Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629) **Siphoning Grip** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The lich legion touches all enemies within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) to drain their life (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions. For each action the lich legion uses, the legion gains 10 temporary Hit Points that last 1 minute.
2d8 void damage
4d8+11 void damage
6d8+13 void damage **Steady Troop Spellcasting** When the lich legion Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), or [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
If a reaction would disrupt the lich legion's spellcasting action, the lich legion attempts a DC 12 flat [check](/Rules.aspx?ID=3292). On a success, the action isn't disrupted.
name: Lich Legion

Though [mitflits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3031) are known as cowardly gremlins, they can be urged into an aggressive fervor by the right leader. Mounted on [giant ticks](/Monsters.aspx?ID=826), [centipedes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=380), and other verminous arthropods, these mitflits overcome their selfloathing to take the fight to their enemies. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Mitflit Vermin Cavalry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3927) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 185 **Perception** +14; darkvision **Languages** [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 4, splash damage 5 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 15 feet, climb 15 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Crawling Stabs** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The mitflits thrust with their shortswords, coordinated with bites from their giant vermin mounts. All enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 piercing damage
2d6+4 piercing damage
2d6+8 piercing damage **Leaping Charge** The mitflit vermin cavalry Leaps up to 30 feet. If it moves at least 15 feet, the cavalry deals 2d6+4 piercing damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) at the end of its movement. **Mounted Troop** Effects that target only animals or only humanoids may not work on the mitflit vermin cavalry, subject to the GM's discretion. **Vengeful Wrath** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) As long as it's not [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), the mitflit vermin cavalry gains a +2 status bonus to the DC of its Crawling Stabs ability against creatures that have previously damaged or tormented it.
name: Mitflit Vermin Cavalry

Many martial artists train to defend themselves and their allies against unwanted aggression, but sometimes the circumstances dictate that even the most peaceful monks must go to war. A cadre of studied monks is capable of dishing out great damage with punches and kicks, as well as focusing their qi into ranged blasts. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Monk Cadre](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3928) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 186 **Perception** +28 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +26 **Will** +25 **HP** 270 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Coordinated Maneuvers** The monk cadre is practiced at putting their foes off-balance. The monks choose [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) and attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to perform that action, comparing the result to the appropriate DC (Fortitude for Grapple and Reposition, Reflex for Disarm and Trip) of each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). This can result in a different degree of success for each target. **Pummeling Punches** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The monks perform well-timed coordinated melee attacks against all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d8 bludgeoning damage
4d8+8 bludgeoning damage
4d8+16 bludgeoning damage **Qi Blast** ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The monks channel their qi into an explosion of energy that affects all creatures in a 10- foot burst within 60 feet. This explosion deals 6d6 force Qi Blast damage with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. When the monk cadre is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385).
name: Monk Cadre

If a large city's waste management system spirals out of control, ofalths can grow and propagate unchecked. These reeking creatures haul tons of trash on their backs and carry a particularly virulent disease. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Ofalth Stampede](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3929) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 186 **Perception** +26; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +28 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Refuse Pile** When an ofalth stampede is not in danger, they can spend 1 minute settling into a 20-foot pile that looks like a heap of garbage. Until the next time they take an action, the troop gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC. A creature that enters the area of the garbage heap or interacts with it must attempt a save against the ofalth stampede's stench. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +24 **Will** +25 **HP** 270 (4 segments, filth wallow) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **Filth Wallow** A trash stampede gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10 when in an area with a high concentration of debris or excrement, such as a refuse heap or sewer.
[**Stench**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=76) (aura, olfactory) 30 feet, DC 33
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Offal Rain** The ofalth stampede hurls a tremendous amount of rotting trash, which rains down in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet. All creatures in the area take 4d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the saving throw are also exposed to wretched weeps. When the troop is reduced to 2 segments, the area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Putrid Pummeling** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The ofalths pummel all enemies in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions. Creatures that fail the saving throw are exposed to wretched weeps.
1d12+3 bludgeoning damage
3d12+7 bludgeoning damage
4d12+10 bludgeoning damage **Wretched Weeps** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 36 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 2d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) every hour and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** 2d6 persistent bleed every hour and enfeebled 2 (1 day)
name: Ofalth Stampede

[Omoxes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2898) are the embodiment of pollution and filth. While they have no true anatomy, they tend to take on mocking humanoid forms—though this distinction is difficult to spotwhen omoxes gather in large groups as part of an attacking force. Such a group often looks like a single roiling mass of slime with multiple torsos jutting up in a military formation. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Omox Slime Pool](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3930) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 186 **Perception** +29; darkvision **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +36 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +9 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Clean Vulnerability** Omoxes embody filth, and they find the concept of cleanliness abhorrent. An omox slime pool subjected to an effect that cleans them takes 4d6 mental damage. They also take this damage the first time each round a creature damaged by an omox slime pool spends actions cleaning off the resultant filth. --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +29 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 315 (4 segments) **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), critical hits, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 15, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **Absorb Weapon** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** A creature hits the omox slime pool with a melee weapon; **Effect** The omoxes attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the creature. On a critical success, the weapon becomes subsumed within the body of an omox rather than falling to the ground. Retrieving the weapon requires a successful DC 45 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to Disarm.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 80 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38 - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **8th** [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) **Rituals** DC 38 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Slime Barrage** The omoxes hurl balls of heavy slime in a 10- foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet. All creatures in the area take 4d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 acid damage (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails the save is mired in the slime, taking a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 38); on a critical failure, the creature is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for the same duration. When the slime pool is reduced to 2 segments, the area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Smothering Grasp** **Requirements** The omox slime pool has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** Omox slime flows onto the creature, completely covering it. The creature must then succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or it becomes [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) and must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439). These effects last as long as the omoxes have the creature grabbed or restrained. **Waves of Sludge** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The omoxes attack all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with slimy tendrils (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that critically fails this saving throw is also [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the slime pool. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+3 bludgeoning damage plus 1d6 acid damage
3d6+12 bludgeoning damage plus 2d6 acid damage
4d6+14 bludgeoning damage plus 3d6 acid damage
name: Omox Slime Pool

[Orc](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=454) raiding parties are feared units whose unrelenting attacks and sheer ferocity make them capable, if not subtle, soldiers. Orc raiders take the duty of carrying their hold's banner into combat with deadly seriousness and will gladly risk grievous injuries to keep their hold's standard flying high. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Orc Raiding Party](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3931) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 187 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 75 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **Ferocious Fall** **Trigger** The orc raiding party is about to lose a segment due to passing a Hit Point threshold; **Effect** The dying orc raiders lash out as they fall. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 1d6+2 piercing damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save); this occurs before the raiding party loses a segment.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Break Through** **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The orc raiders exploit a gap in enemy lines. The orc raiding party [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice; it can pass through spaces of Medium or smaller creatures but can't end its movement in them. All enemies whose spaces the orc raiding party passed through or were adjacent to at any point during their movement take 1d6+2 piercing damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who critically fails this save is also pushed 5 feet away from the orc raiding party. Break Through damages each creature only once. **Iron Rain** The orc raiders launch a multitude of javelins at foes in a deadly volley. This volley is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet that deals 3d6 piercing damage with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. When the orc raiding party is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Rip Them Up** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The orc raiders batter all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with coordinated knuckle dagger strikes (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+2 piercing damage
2d6+5 piercing damage
3d6+7 piercing damage
name: Orc Raiding Party

markdown:
Due to the inherently chaotic nature of [proteans](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=457), many would assume they are incapable of cooperation. However, they are cunning enough to be able to unite in the face of danger or at the behest of more powerful beings, sometimes acting as a group called a protean tumult. Such an association is comprising of dozens of proteans of different shapes and sizes, many of whom continually alter their forms from moment to moment. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Protean Tumult](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3932) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 188 **Perception** +21; darkvision, entropy sense (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +20 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Entropy Sense** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) A protean tumult can anticipate the most likely presence of a creature through a supernatural insight into chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants it the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. Veil of privacy prevents a creature from being detected via entropy sense automatically (without a counteract check). --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 210 (4 segments, [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 8) **Resistances** precision 8, protean anatomy 12 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The vital organs of each individual protean in the troop shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the protean tumult takes [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the tumult takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The tumult is immune to [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effects unless it is a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), the tumult automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet, swim 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85), [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Chaos Strike** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The protean tumult chooses [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915), [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916), or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920); the damage dealt by its Claws, Jaws, and Tails is treated as that material for 1 minute or until it uses Chaos Strike again. **Chaos Flux** A protean tumult is less organized and more vicious than most troops. It can move into other creatures' spaces, and other creatures can move into its spaces. Its spaces are difficult terrain to non-protean creatures. A creature that willingly moves into a protean tumult's space takes 1d12+1 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save); a creature takes this damage only once per round. **Claws, Jaws, and Tails** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The tumult viciously attacks each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d12+1 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage
2d12+10 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage
3d12+11 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage **Stupefying Swipe** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The protean tumult makes their way across the battlefield. It [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). At the end of this movement, they lash out at the enemy with tentacles and other blunt body parts, dealing 2d12+10 bludgeoning damage in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who fails this save is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 2 rounds (stupefied 3 on a critical failure).
name: Protean Tumult

markdown:
Navigating the deserts of Golarion requires trained individuals and often specialized mounts to keep them safe. The Qadiran camel corps are one such example of those who embark on long patrols, hunt down brigands, and dispatch any natural threats that might trouble travelers. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Qadiran Camel Corps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3933) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 189 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Kelish](/Languages.aspx?ID=38) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Desert-Adapted Troop** A camel corps is well-adapted to heat and deserts. They treat environmental heat as if it was one step less severe, and if the camel mounts have eaten and drank their fill, the corps can Subsist for 1 week without needing to attempt Survival checks. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 90 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 35 feet, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Dust Storm** **Requirements** The camel corps is in a desert or similar region; **Effect** The camel corps [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), whipping up sand into a short-lived storm that obscures the surrounding region. Until the end of their next turn, the camel corps is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from all creatures more than 15 feet away, and all creatures more than 15 feet away are concealed to the camel corps. **Mounted Troop** Effects that target only animals or only humanoids may not work on the camel corps, subject to the GM's discretion. **Reflective Arrows** Adjusting to the angle of the sun, the camel riders draw their shortbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of an arcing volley. This volley is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet that deals 3d6 piercing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). In areas of bright light, such as outside during the day, the targets take a –1 circumstance penalty to the save. When the camel corps is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Scimitar Assault** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The camel corps engages in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+1 slashing damage
2d6+7 slashing damage
2d6+11 slashing damage **Trailblazing Stride** While moving on land, the Qadiran camel corps ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain.
name: Qadiran Camel Corps

Urban combat specialists with plenty of tricks up their sleeves, this unit of ratfolk is known for using poisoned weapons and dirty tricks. Ratfolk shank squads are often mercenaries with loyalty only to the group who is paying them the most. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Ratfolk Shank Squad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3934) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 189 **Perception** +15; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +17 **Str** +0 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +18 **Will** +12 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Dirty Tricks** The ratfolk feint and trip up their foes in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), attempting a single [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check and comparing the result to each target's Reflex DC. On a success, the target is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (clumsy 2 on a critical success) until they take an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to remove the condition. **Poisoned Bolts** The shank squad draws hand crossbows to fire poisoned bolts in a volley. This volley is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet that deals 2d6 piercing damage and 2d6 poison damage with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. When the shank squad is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Shank 'Em** Frequency once per round; **Effect** The shank squad makes a coordinated attack with shivs and daggers against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 slashing damage and 1d4 precision damage
2d6+3 slashing damage and 2d4 precision damage
2d6+6 slashing damage and 3d4 precision damage
name: Ratfolk Shank Squad

[Redcaps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=348) are sadistic fey known for their merciless bloodlust. When they gather into troops, they goad each other to greater heights of violence. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Redcap Brigade](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3935) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 189 **Perception** +19; low-light vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +17 **HP** 165 (4 segments, [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 20) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10, divine revulsion, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Divine Revulsion** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) If a redcap brigade sees a creature brandish a religious symbol of a deity (which requires an Interact action by that creature) or cast a divine spell while wearing a religious symbol, the troop must attempt a DC 26 Will save. They then become temporarily immune to all brandished religious symbols for 10 minutes. **Critical Success** The troop is unaffected.
**Success** The troop is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The troop is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The troop is frightened 3.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 50 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Blood Soak** **Trigger** The redcap brigade loses its first segment, causes another troop to lose its first segment, or is otherwise exposed to copious amounts of blood; Effect The redcap brigade gains a status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. The bonus is +2 if they spend one action on Bloody Reaping, +6 if they spend two actions, or +8 if they spend 3 actions. They gain a +4 status bonus to damage rolls with Bowl Over and Stomp. **Bloody Reaping** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The redcaps in the brigade wildly swing their halberds and sickles at each enemy in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d10+2 slashing
2d10+9 slashing
3d10+10 slashing **Bowl Over and Stomp** The redcap brigade Strides; they can pass through spaces of Medium or smaller creatures, but can't end their movement in them. All enemies whose spaces the redcap brigade passed through take 4d8 bludgeoning damage and must attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save. Bowl Over and Stomp damages each creature only once.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage. If it is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), it also takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is knocked prone, and takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage. **Deadly Swipes** **Trigger** The redcap brigade drops a creature to 0 Hit Points with Bloody Reaping; **Effect** The redcap brigade performs an additional one-action Bloody Reaping, ignoring the once per round frequency limitation. This does not deal damage to the triggering creature.
name: Redcap Brigade
