Fire scamps are playful and excitable pranksters from the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31) that have little concept of moderation. When they gather into flocks, unintentional arson is the inevitable result. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Scamp Inferno](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3936) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 190 **Perception** +14; darkvision, smoke vision **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +13 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +4 Smoke Vision The scamp inferno ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +17 **Will** +14 **HP** 90 (4 segments, [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 6 (while touching fire)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=70), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, cold 8, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 8 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 21, attack +15 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **3rd** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) **Burning Bites** The scamp inferno attacks with a flurry of tiny teeth and flickering flames. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 piercing damage and 1d4 fire damage
2d6+2 piercing damage and 2d4 fire damage
2d6+4 piercing damage and 3d4 fire damage **Flame Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The scamp inferno breathes flames in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that deals 3d6 fire damage to each creature within the area (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save also take 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The scamp inferno can't use Flame Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
Other Elemental Scamp Troops In General, All Elemental Scamps Are A Bit Mischievous, And Equally As Likely To Gather In Masses To Wreak (Often Accidental) Havoc. You Can Alter The Scamp Inferno Stat Block Above To Represent Troops Of Other Elemental Scamps. Replace The Fire Trait With The Trait Listed In Parentheses, And Apply The Other Adjustments.
  • Scamp Avalanche ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) Replace Smoke Vision With [Tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (Imprecise) 30 Feet change language to Petran; the troop's fast healing works underground; remove immunity to fire and weakness to cold; the troop gains a [burrow Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2348) of 20 feet; replace the innate spells with 3rd [_one with stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1619) and Cantrips (3rd) [_scatter scree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2021); replace Burning Bites with Stone Slams, dealing bludgeoning and poison damage instead of piercing and fire damage; replace Flame Breath with Scree Breath, dealing bludgeoning damage and no persistent fire damage.
  • **Scamp Flood** ([water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) Remove smoke vision; change language to [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); the troop's fast healing works underwater; remove immunity to fire and weakness to cold; add resistance to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) and fire at the same value as the weakness values; the troop gains a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) of 25 feet; replace the innate spells with 2nd [_acid grip_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436) and 1st [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476); replace Burning Bites with Drenching Claws, dealing slashing and acid damage instead of piercing and fire damage; replace Flame Breath with Acid Breath, dealing acid damage and no [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
  • **Scamp Shrapnel** ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) Remove smoke vision; change language to Talican; the troop's fast healing works while touching metal; remove immunity to fire and weakness to cold; add resistance to electricity at the same value as the weakness values; replace the innate spells with 2nd [_leaden steps_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2005) and Cantrips (3rd) [_live wire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2008); replace Burning Bites with Metallic Claws, dealing slashing and persistent bleed damage instead of piercing and fire damage; replace Flame Breath with Shrapnel Breath, dealing slashing damage and persistent bleed damage.
  • **Scamp Tangle** ([wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) Remove smoke vision; change language to [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); the troop's fast healing works while touching plants or trees; remove immunity to fire and replace weakness to cold with weakness to fire and weakness to slashing; replace the innate spells with 2nd [_oaken resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617) (self only) and Cantrips (3rd) [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713); replace Burning Bites with Thorny Claws, dealing piercing and persistent bleed damage instead of piercing and fire damage; replace Flame Breath with Pollen Breath, dealing poison damage and no persistent fire damage.
  • **Scamp Whirlwind** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) Replace smoke vision with fog vision, which offers the same benefit in fog and mist; change language to [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); the troop's fast healing works in open air; remove immunity to fire and weakness to cold; the troop increases its [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) to 40 feet; replace the innate spells with 2nd [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) and 1st [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550); replace Burning Bites with Sonic Slams, dealing bludgeoning damage and sonic damage instead of piercing and fire damage; replace Flame Breath with Sirocco Breath, dealing slashing damage and pushing back a target who fails the save by 10 feet (instead of dealing persistent fire damage).
name: Scamp Inferno

[Sinspawn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3192), monstrosities of twisted humanoid flesh, were created ages ago by the runelord Alaznist to serve as shock troops. Hateful even toward their own kind, sinspawn usually band together only in small, isolated cults, although powerful beings are occasionally able to coerce greater numbers of these slavering horrors to join forces as uniquely bloodthirsty and destructive mobs. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Sinswarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3937) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 191 **Perception** +19; darkvision, sin scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Sin Scent** A sinswarm can smell creatures reflecting any one of the seven primary sins as the scent ability. The GM determines which creatures are appropriately sinful.
 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +18 **Will** +15 **HP** 150 (4 segments) **Immunities** controlled **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Reactive Attack** **Trigger** A creature within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the sinswarm uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The sinswarm lashes out at the triggering creature, dealing 2d8+9 piercing or slashing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the creature critically fails the save and the trigger was a manipulate action, that action is disrupted.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Sinful Assault** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The sinswarm makes a coordinated attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 piercing or slashing damage
2d8+9 piercing or slashing damage plus sinful bite
3d8+11 piercing or slashing damage plus sinful bite **Sinful Bite** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature bitten by a sinspawn must attempt a DC 28 Will save as it is assailed by sinful thoughts. The sinswarm can't inflict the same sin effect on multiple targets in the same round until it has inflicted all seven sins at least once.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and takes one of the following additional effects, chosen by the GM: [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 for 1 minute (envy), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (gluttony), [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 for 1 minute (greed), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 1 minute (lust), clumsy 1 and enfeebled 1 for 1 minute (pride), –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds for 1 minute (sloth), or drained 1 and enfeebled 1 for 1 minute (wrath).
name: Sinswarm

While some of these skeletons are far from whole, they can still pose a significant threat. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Skeleton Mob](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3938) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 191 **Perception** +11; darkvision **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** -5 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 90 (4 segments, void healing (page 217)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 7, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 7, fire 7, piercing 7, slashing 7 **Weaknesses** area damage 7, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 7 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Ossuary Storm** The skeleton mob hurls skulls and fragments of bone in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet. This attack deals 3d6 piercing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the skeleton mob is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Rattling Bones** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The skeletons engage using their claws and broken bones to attack each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

3d4+10 piercing or slashing damageto 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst.

[Vanth ](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3148) [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) are eternal guardians of the cycle of life and death. When souls are threatened by fiends or other malevolent forces, they gather together to cut the threat down. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Vanth Guardian Flock](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3939) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 192 **Perception** +23; darkvision, [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +25 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 240 (4 segments) **Immunities** [death effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 15, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 15 **Weaknesses** area damage 12, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 12 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet, DC 30
**Reactive Relocation** ([teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** A creature hits the guardian flock with an attack roll; **Effect** After the attack roll is resolved, the troop pools dimensional magic to rapidly change their position. They cast 4th-rank [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), except their range is limited to 40 feet.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 33 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **6th** [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (×3), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) (×3), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Guardians' Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** three times per day; Effect The guardian flock bestows a curse upon all enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) by touching them with their scythes. Each affected creature must attempt a DC 33 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Guardians' Curse for 24 hours.
**Success** The target feels a momentary shudder of doom and is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute by the distracting sensation.
**Failure** The target becomes morose and glum as it accepts its own inevitable fate. For 1 hour, the target is stupefied 2. Each time the target gains the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition, the stupefied condition value increases by 1, to a maximum value of stupefied 4.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect is permanent. **Harvest the Wicked** **Frequency** once per round; Effect The vanths swing their scythes in a coordinated melee attack. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions. The slashing damage is treated as adamantine, cold iron, and silver.
1d10+1 slashing damage plus 1d6 shepherd's touch
2d10+3 slashing damage plus 3d6 shepherd's touch
2d10+7 slashing damage plus 4d6 shepherd's touch **Shepherd's Touch** The physical damage dealt by the guardian flock's Harvest the Wicked ability is treated as coming from a weapon with a [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune. In addition, the vanths deal the listed damage as void damage to living creatures or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).
name: Vanth Guardian Flock

Battle-tested Ulfen barbarians, hunters, and warriors gather into an elite fighting unit who specialize in offering protection to their leaders in battle as well as protecting those who can afford their services. Traveling far and wide, single troops or entire companies can easily find work based on their reputations, from serving as bodyguards in small private armies to taking on specialized jobs. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Viking Guard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3940) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 193 **Perception** +21 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Skald](/Languages.aspx?ID=42) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 +2 status to all saves vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **HP** 195 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Sacrifice** **Requirements** The viking guard has a charge, and that creature is adjacent to the viking guard; **Trigger** The viking guard's charge takes Hit Point damage; **Effect** The viking guard's charge takes half damage, and the viking guard takes the remainder of the damage.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Berserker Strikes** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Battle axes in hand, the viking guard engages in a coordinated melee attack against enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+3 slashing damage
2d8+12 slashing damage
3d8+15 slashing damage **Guard Charge** The viking guard designates an ally it can see to be its charge. The charge gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC, Reflex saves, and saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) when they are adjacent to the viking guard. Further, this allows the viking guard to use its Sacrifice and Shield Wall actions. A viking guard can have only one charge at a time, and if it designates a new charge, the old one loses all benefits. If the viking guard's charge is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the viking guard must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2; this is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), fear, and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect. **Shield Wall** **Requirements** The viking guard has a charge, and that creature is adjacent to the viking guard; **Effect** Raising shields, the viking guard Strides up to twice its Speed as it protects its charge. The viking guard gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against reactions triggered by this movement. The viking guard's charge can Stride the same distance as a reaction, moving with the troop to maintain the bonuses from Guard Charge during this movement.
name: Viking Guard

[Vordines](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2906) are the foot soldiers of Hell, dispatched in vast armies from the iron city of Dis to guard Hell's uppermost layers and crush the enemies of the archdevils who rule there. Indefatigable and pitiless, a legion of vordines strikes with a ruthless precision that commands fear and envy throughout the multiverse in equal measure. Although they usually serve at the command of more powerful devils, such as the tyrannical nessari, every legion also observes a unique internal hierarchy that enables another vordine to assume command at a moment's notice should it become necessary. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Vordine Legion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3941) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 193 **Perception** +19; greater darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +19 **Will** +16 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 180 (4 segments) **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10, splash damage 10 **Reactive Attack** **Trigger** A creature within the vordine legion's reach uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The creature takes 2d8+11 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save); this damage has the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits. If the creature critically fails its saving throw and the trigger was a manipulate action, the legion disrupts that action.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) **Burning March** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The vordine legion Strides, leaving an orderly pattern of burning hoofprints in each square they enter. The hoofprints continue to burn for 1 minute. Any creature on the ground that begins its turn in, or enters a square with, burning hoofprints takes 2d8 fire damage. **Impaling Barrage** ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The vordine legion releases a hail of tridents. This hail is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 40 feet that deals 4d8 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the saving throw are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the start of the vordine legion's next turn (clumsy 2 on a critical failure). When the vordines are reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Tines of Dis** ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The vordines of the legion make coordinated melee attacks with their tridents. Each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 piercing damage
2d8+11 piercing damage
3d8+14 piercing damage
name: Vordine Legion

markdown:
[Wights](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=163) are malevolent undead, bent on desecrating the holy and determined to cause suffering to all living creatures. A lone wight is dangerous enough, but when compelled to act together, they are a force to be reckoned with. A wight battalion has the potential to curse entire armies, increasing their numbers until they become an unholy tide. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Wight Battalion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3942) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 194 **Perception** +18; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 **HP** 150 (4 segments, fueled by spite, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Weaknesses** area damage 7, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 7 **Final Grudge** **Trigger** The wight battalion is about to lose a segment due to Hit Point damage; **Effect** The wights strike out as they fall. Each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 2d4 piercing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This occurs before the battalion loses a segment.
**Fueled by Spite** Each time a creature loses Hit Points due to the wight battalion's corrupting spite curse, the battalion gains 6 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round.
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Corrupting Spite** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The wight battalion's attacks inflict a curse that makes a creature grow weak and spiteful. A living humanoid that dies while under this curse rises as a [wight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3239) after 1d4 rounds, controlled by the wight battalion that inflicted the curse. This new wight can't inflict corrupting spite and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2. If the creating wight battalion dies or after roughly a month of existence, the new wight becomes autonomous and becomes a normal wight; **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** drained 2 and doesn't treat any creatures as allies (1 round); **Stage 3** As stage 2, except drained 3 (1 round); **Stage 4** As stage 2, except drained 4 (1 round). **Hateful Daggers** The wights coordinate melee attacks with the daggers they were buried with. Each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) attempts a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions. On a failed save, the creature is also exposed to corrupting spite.
2d4 piercing damage
4d4+8 piercing damage
4d4+14 piercing damage
name: Wight Battalion

Though [wolf](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=485) packs can be a menace to farming communities that rely on livestock, they prefer not to go after humanoids or other dangerous foes. However, hungry packs don't have the luxury of being cautious. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Wolf Pack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3943) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 194 **Perception** +17; low-light vision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +11 **HP** 90 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 [**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 35 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Harry Prey** The wolf pack focuses all their efforts on biting a single adjacent foe, who takes 4d6+8 piercing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the creature fails this saving throw, the wolf pack can immediately attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) the creature. **Pack Hunt** The wolves work together to fell their opponents. Each enemy within a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) attempts a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature who is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 for this attack. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+1 piercing damage
2d6+7 piercing damage
2d6+11 piercing damage

Though most [xulgath](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=486) settlements can be found in the Darklands, these reptilian humanoids sometimes venture to the surface with ill intent. When gathered into an army, xulgaths tear through innocent communities in the name of whatever leader they serve. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Xulgath Army](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3944) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 194 **Perception** +14; darkvision **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 99 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **[Stench](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=76)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 30 feet, DC 24
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Club Offensive** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The xulgaths make coordinated melee attacks against each enemy in 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d6+1 bludgeoning damage
2d6+7 bludgeoning damage
2d6+12 bludgeoning damage **Javelin Barrage** The xulgaths draw javelins and launch a coordinated barrage at range. This barrage is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet that deals 3d6 piercing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the xulgath army is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Rend Flesh** The xulgaths concentrate their attacks on a single adjacent enemy, clawing and biting with abandon. That creature takes 3d4+5 slashing damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a failed save, the creature also takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
name: Xulgath Army

[Xulgaths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=486) have been breeding and training [dinosaurs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=391) for millennia, and the bonds between them are as familiar and strong as that between any other humanoid and their domesticated companion of choice. While most of the creatures are used primarily as beasts of burden, elite xulgath soldiers known as roughriders train the most vicious and aggressive dinosaurs from numerous species to serve as vicious and terrifying mounts, capable of punching through defensive lines and smashing fortifications to splinters as easily as they scatter enemy soldiers. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Xulgath Dinosaur Cavalry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3945) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 195 **Perception** +23; low-light vision, scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Dinosaur Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +20 **Will** +23 **HP** 240 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **[Stench](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=76)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 30 feet, DC 33
[**Troop Defenses**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 30 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Javelin Barrage** The xulgaths draw javelins and launch a coordinated barrage at range. This barrage is a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet that deals 4d8 piercing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). When the cavalry is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Mounted Troop** Effects that target only animals, dinosaurs, or humanoids may not work on the cavalry, subject to the GM's discretion. **Spears, Teeth, and Horns** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The cavalry makes a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
2d6+2 piercing damage
4d6+10 piercing damage
5d6+15 piercing damage **Trample** The cavalry [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to double its Speed and can move through the spaces of Large or smaller creatures, Trampling each creature whose space it enters. The cavalry can attempt to Trample the same creature only once in a single use of Trample. Each Trampled creature takes 2d6+2 bludgeoning damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
name: Xulgath Dinosaur Cavalry

A surprise raid by a swarm of the insectile [zecui](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3248) can easily wipe out an entire village overnight. When they don't devour their victims immediately, zecuis typically implant them with their own larvae and bury them in sprawling mass graves, where the developing zecui horde can lie dormant for years before suddenly erupting to the surface to wreak havoc on nearby settlements. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Zecui Horde](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3946) **Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 195 **Perception** +21; darkvision **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +24 **Will** +18 **HP** 195 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet, climb 20 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Harden Chitin** The zecuis fuse their chitin into black metallic shells. The horde gains resistance 10 to all damage (except [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) until they next take a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action. **Mandible Frenzy** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The horde makes a vicious bite attack against each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 piercing damage
2d8+12 piercing damage
3d8+15 piercing damage **Mucus Deluge** The horde spits a volley of larva-infested mucus as a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 30 feet. Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 27 Reflex save or be stuck to the nearest surface, [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until they [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 30). Any creature so immobilized is exposed to zecui larvae at the end of each of its turns. When the zecui horde is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Subterranean Ambush** **Requirements** The zecui horde has burrowed into an ambush position just beneath a surface of dirt, sand, or a similar loose material; **Effect** The horde bursts from the ground and moves up to its Speed. The horde deals 1d8+2 piercing damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to each enemy in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) at the end of this movement. **Zecui Larvae** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Stage 1** visible lumps as the larvae move but no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** drained 3 and controlled by the zecui larva (1 day); **Stage 5** the creature dies and an adult [zecui](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3248) can emerge from the corpse as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action.
name: Zecui Horde

<title level="1" pfs="">[Riptide Octopus Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3947)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Riptide octopuses have adapted to these isolated hunting grounds with their strength, pulling themselves hundreds of feet across dry land to check their territory's pools for prey. Under most circumstances, such mollusks are solitary. However, massive scavenging opportunities like a beached whale or a wounded mammal can draw hundreds of these creatures to one place.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 14

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Riptide Octopus Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3947)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=533" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=707" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 84

**Perception** +6; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +4

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +3

**Int** -4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 14

</row>

**Immunities**
[grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), precision, [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78)

**Resistances**
physical 5 (except slashing)

**Weaknesses**
area damage 3, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, swim 30 feet

**Camouflage**   In wet environments like open water, tide pools, and even wet ship decks, a riptide octopus swarm can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62).

**Compression**   A riptide octopus swarm can move through a gap at least 6 inches wide without [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372) and can Squeeze through a gap at least 1 inch wide.

**Swarming Bites** <actions string="Single Action" />  Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 1d4 piercing damage and must attempt a DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save takes 1d4 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as octopuses cling to and continue biting the creature.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Eater Of Toes</title>

The riptide octopus didn't get its name from living in tidal zones but from being more stubborn than a riptide pulling someone out to sea. Beachcombers know not to dip their toes into tidal pools lest a riptide octopus mistake them for prey, and many bear scars that remind them of the dangers of ignoring such advice. Dislodging a hungry riptide octopus sometimes takes extreme measures in the form of a crowbar; fortunately, the octopus's skin is sensitive to fermented fluids like vinegar and wine, and dousing the creature with such concoctions usually encourages it to detach and flee.
</aside>
name: Riptide Octopus Swarm

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bristled Reef Shark](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3948)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Small enough to slip through labyrinthine corals yet large enough to overpower potential prey, bristled reef sharks prowl coastal reefs at night. By day, they're gregarious creatures that congregate in large groups and communicate with each other by performing dance-like maneuvers. On occasion, the sharks team up to drive away predators or mob large prey; more often, they dart for cover amid the corals. Those that aren't fast enough to avoid being swallowed by orcas and larger sharks flare their dorsal spines, forcing most predators to cough up the bristled shark who quickly flees to safety.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=533" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 84

**Perception** +7; blood scent, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +5, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Blood Scent** The shark can smell blood in the water from up to 1 mile away.

</column>

---

**AC** 16 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Bristle** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The bristled reef shark becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or otherwise [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in a creature's grasp; **Effect** The shark flares its bristles, dealing 1d6 piercing damage to the triggering creature ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex DC 17). If the creature critically fails, the shark can also try to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as part of this reaction.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** swim 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +5,
**Damage** 1d8+1 piercing

**Compression**   When a bristled reef shark successfully [Squeezes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), it moves though the tight space at half speed (or full speed on a critical success).

**Reef Rake** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The shark's last action was a successful jaws Strike, and the target is adjacent to a solid surface like a wall or reef; **Effect** The shark attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) the target into the adjacent surface. If successful, the target is thrown into the surface and takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage. If the surface is jagged (such as a reef), target instead takes 2d6 slashing damage and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

</column>
name: Bristled Reef Shark

With the physique of a giant lobster and the artistic sensibilities of a hermit crab, a coralclaw relies on ambush to catch prey. As their name suggests, they adorn their shells by secreting an adhesive and attaching broken corals to blend in with their local reefs. Older coralclaws often foster barnacles, anemones, and even living coral on their back to enhance the disguise. Once properly adorned, the coralclaw finds a hollow to hide within and wait for prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Coralclaw](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3949) **Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 85 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +11 **Will** +5 **HP** 30 **Coral Camouflage** In reefs and rocky marine environments, a coralclaw can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62).
**Dislodged Armor** Once the coralclaw is reduced to half its Hit Points, its AC decreases by 2, it gains a +5 circumstance bonus to its Speeds, and it loses coral camouflage. A coralclaw can repair its coral armor with 4 hours of work.
**Shuffler** A coralclaw struggles to pivot quickly, leaving its hind limbs and tail vulnerable. When the coralclaw is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), it gains weakness 2 to physical damage except against effects for which the coralclaw has [standard cover or greater cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459). --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 15 feet **Melee** crusher claw +11, **Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** piercer claw +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing plus 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ambushing Snap** **Requirements** The coralclaw is hiding in corals and a creature that hasn't detected it is within 25 feet; **Effect** The coralclaw moves up to its swim Speed + 10 feet toward the triggering creature. When the creature is within reach, the coralclaw makes a melee Strike against it. The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to this Strike. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d10 bludgeoning, DC 18 **Crushing Drag** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Requirements** The coralclaw is [grabbing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restraining](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) a creature; **Effect** The coralclaw Constricts the creature, extends the grabbed or restrained condition until the end of the coralclaw's next turn, and attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) the creature.
Coralclaw Caches When a coralclaw molts, it leaves behind its coral-covered shell. These husks provide temporary homes for various fish, and they warn divers that an ambush predator dwells in the area. In addition, these shells are often studded with not just valuable corals and shells, but coins, glassware dredged from forgotten shipwrecks, and even the occasional magical item!
name: Coralclaw

markdown:
An unusual breed of cervid, Iblydan hinds are closer to the size of a bull or a moose than the average deer, often ten feet or more in height. They have smooth, pale coats, hooves formed of bronze, and both the males and the females of the species bear antlers of pure gold. Iblydan hinds are known for their exceptional speed and agility as well as their intelligence, making them extraordinarily elusive in the wild. There's some debate as to the origins of the Iblydan hind. Some scholars believe the species came to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35) through a long-ago planar breach, noting how the hinds' unusual characteristics are similar to the unpredictable evolutions of creatures from that plane. Others point to historical records that indicate the hind was purposefully bred by ancient druidic circles, though the purpose of their creation has been lost to time. Regardless of their pedigree, Iblydan hinds are a key symbol of Iblydan culture, often depicted in art and heraldry, with various superstitions surrounding them. A recurring element in hero-god stories is for an Iblydan hind to lead a hero-god to the correct path when they become lost, and it's said that a hunter who glimpses a hind in the forest is assured of a long life and good fortune. Due to the hinds' near-sacred status throughout Iblydos, slaying one is banned by all the city-states and punishable by exile, even death. In tribute to the hinds' fire breath, a common punishment for poachers is a trial by fire; if they survive the flames, they're allowed to live in exile. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Iblydan Hind](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3950) **Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 86 **Perception** +11; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 30 feet **Languages** [Iblydosi](/Languages.aspx?ID=182), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Fleet of Hoof** An Iblydan hind ignores non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) or greater difficult terrain when Striding or Stepping. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +14 **Will** +8 **HP** 62 **Evade Ensnarement** **Trigger** The hind is targeted with an effect that would give it the [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) condition; **Effect** The hind Steps, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its saving throw or increasing its DC by 2 against the effect. --- **Speed** 50 feet **Melee** antlers +13, **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing **Melee** hoof +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 19 - **Constant (2nd)** [Vanishing Tracks](/Spells.aspx?ID=1737) **Fire Breath** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The hind snorts a 15-foot cone of fire, dealing 5d6 fire damage (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The hind can't use fire breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Bright-Horned Flare** ([Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The hind tosses its head, causing its golden horns to reflect the light. All creatures within 30 feet of the hind must attempt a DC 18 Fortitude save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 18
Chasing The Hind Chasing down and catching an Iblydan hind is a well-known rite of passage for Iblydan heroes and hero-gods, who often use this as a test of their speed and stamina. “Hind catching” is also a common game at festivals, with a dog or deer standing in for the hind, and golden antlers are a popular hair accessory at these events. Heroes aim to catch and release the hind without harming it; however, some who pursue Iblydan hinds do so with darker motivations, as the creatures' golden antlers fetch a high price on the black market.
name: Iblydan Hind

Though sometimes dubbed “lesser [phoenixes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137),” the pyrefowl resembles the legendary firebirds in appearance and abilities, yet any direct connection between the two species is entirely anecdotal. A typical specimen resembles a bustard or peafowl with brown, red, and orange feathers. When it's excited, the feathers flicker with incandescence, and an irate pyrefowl can create sparks with a click of its beak or blow burning embers down at a foe with a wingbeat. Pyrefowl travel in small flocks for protection, and some hunt collectively by fanning out in a crescent and beating their wings. The resulting heat wave scatters insects and small mammals into the waiting beaks of other fowl. When threatened, a flock might turn this tactic against aggressors, even starting a brush fire to chase off intruders. Though they often roost in high places like rooftops in cities or on rocky bluffs and are certainly capable of flight, pyrefowl spend much of their time on the ground standing on their sturdy legs. Though these creatures are protected in some places, in others they're seen as a dangerous nuisance for their tendency to start or spread fires. In these locales, pyrefowl nests are hunted down, and the birds are either scattered or slain. Their beautiful feathers are sometimes harvested for artistic purposes, often decorating fancy clothing and adornments. Pyrefowl lay few eggs, far less than a typical chicken or duck, and in a few regions their eggs are seen as a delicacy and prized by elite cooks who feature them in baked egg dishes or incorporate them into baked goods. Most note the warm and smoky taste, with a slight hint of almond. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 17 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 [Pyrefowl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3951) **Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 87 **Perception** +7; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); (cannot speak) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +6, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +8 **Will** +6 **HP** 24 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 3 **Smoldering Convalescence** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** When a pyrefowl dies, its wounds ignite and smolder as its body tries to heal. After 1d4 rounds, the pyrefowl attempts a DC 9 flat check. The check automatically fails if the smoldering pyrefowl is doused with water, sand, or other fire suppressant while recovering.
**Critical Success** As success, but the pyrefowl regains 10 Hit Points, Stands, and deals 1d6 fire damage to adjacent creatures and objects (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
**Success** The pyrefowl is returned to life with 5 Hit Points.
**Failure** The pyrefowl remains dead. --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** peck +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6+1 piercing plus Pyrebite **Flame Flutter** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The pyrefowl Steps before or after using this ability. With a flap of its wings, the pyrefowl blows sparks in a 15-foot cone, dealing 2d4 fire damage to creatures in the area ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex DC 16). **Pyrebite** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** The pyrefowl hits a target with its peck Strike on its turn; **Effect** The target must attempt a DC 16 Reflex save or take 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
Fortune And Fireproofing Thanks to their association with its patron phoenix, the city-state of Pol-Bailax extends legal protections to its resident pyrefowl. Harassing or killing the birds is prohibited, though licensed wardens are allowed to harvest naturally shed feathers. Pyrefowl roosting atop one's roof is said to bring good luck to a household, so the Bailaxians build roofs out of clay tiles, slate, or other fire-resistant materials to protect themselves while hoping to lure luck to their homes with small, built-in troughs of grains.
name: Pyrefowl

Speiroikos are born of an unusual ritual in which dragon teeth are sown in the ground, giving rise to a legion of fierce warriors formed of earth. Magic items to summon speiroikos also exist and, on rare occasions, they can arise spontaneously after a dragon is slain, and its body decomposes into the soil. At first glance, a speiroikos looks like a normal humanoid, though close inspection reveals their skin to have a clay-like texture and their teeth to be draconic fangs. They eagerly follow the one who crafted them into battle and obey their creator's orders without question. One might expect them to be perfect soldiers, but they're prone to fighting among themselves if provoked. Typically, speiroikos last only as long as there's a battle to fight, disintegrating back into dirt once hostilities conclude. Some canny creators manage to prolong their existence by hurrying them from one battle to another. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Speiroikos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3952) **Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 88 **Perception** +9 **Languages** [Iblydosi](/Languages.aspx?ID=182) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +9 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +8 **Will** +6 **HP** 50 (4 segments) **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), sickened, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** area damage 3, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 3, diverted fury **Diverted Fury** The speiroikos' aggression can be redirected against their own numbers. A creature within 30 feet of the speiroikos can take an action to throw a small object such as a rock among the troop, attempting a DC 17 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check; this action has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. On a success, the troop deals its two- action Dory Strike damage to itself on its next round, taking no other actions.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; troop movement **Dragonhide** The speiroikos channel the power of their progenitor dragon to grow thick, scaly hides. Until the end of their next turn, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC, and they reduce their weaknesses to area damage and splash damage by 2. **Dory Strike** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The speiroikos make a melee attack against each enemy in its space and in a 10-foot emanation (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6 piercing damage
1d6+6 piercing damage
2d6+6 piercing damage **Kestros Volley** The speiroikos let loose a flurry of heavy darts flung from slings. The volley is a 10-foot burst within 60 feet that deals 1d8 piercing damage (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) plus 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). When the speiroikos are reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Troop Movement** Whenever a troop moves, you move one of its segments and the other segments follow behind it. At the end of the movement, you can group the other segments adjacent to the one you moved as you see fit, provided none of them moves farther than the moving segment. If you choose not to move the troop any distance, you can instead reshape the position of all the segments as long as one stays in place.
Wandering Speiroikos On rare occasions, a surviving member of a speiroikos troop will maintain its existence after battle, gaining full autonomy; this occurs more often in those created spontaneously. Such survivors often experience an existential crisis, having previously existed only to fight. Some wander the world seeking combat, while others attempt to blend in with humanoid societies and find new aspirations.
name: Speiroikos

<title level="1" pfs="">[Torcheater](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3953)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When bright lights or large fires illuminate terrible crimes, the perpetrators' shadows can animate and take on a life of their own. However, such an existence is temporary; the spirit slowly fades over the course of days or weeks unless it can rest in shadows and consume light, both of which restore its shadowy body.

 Known as torcheaters, these spirits often haunt settlements and play pranks on nighttime travelers by stealing their lamps. They're also a threat to travelers, especially those traversing the roads after dark. An encounter with a torcheater on a dark and winding road through the mountains can be deadly if they snuff the traveler's light. However, torcheaters can't stand being the subject of pranks in turn. If caught or taunted, a torcheater can quickly turn violent and attack their tormentor.

 In rare places of near-constant light where shadows don't fall, a torcheater can still exist in a sort of limbo where they're aware of their surroundings but unable manifest enough to affect the world around them. They can only hope for some shade to fall across them so they can then begin to restore their shadowy forms.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 89

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +6, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5, physical 5 (except bronze)

**Shelter Within the Shadow Cast** When a torcheater succeeds at a saving throw against a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) effect, it gets a critical success instead.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet; shadow stairs

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +11 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 piercing plus 1d4 cold

**Inhale Light** <actions string="Single Action or Two Actions" /> ([Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The torcheater sharply inhales to consume a source of light within its reach, such as a torch's flame, a [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) spell, or another effect no larger than a human. It automatically extinguishes a non-magical flame, and it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) a magical [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) effect at 2nd-rank with a counteract modifier of +8. If the torcheater successfully counteracts an effect, it gains a [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) of 10 feet, a +5 foot status bonus to Speed, and [fast healing 2](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) until the end of its next turn, plus an additional number of rounds equal to the counteracted effect's rank. During this time, the creature's body solidifies, reducing its resistance to physical damage to 2.

 The torcheater can spend two actions to instead affect a light source it can perceive within 30 feet.

**Shadow Stairs**   A torcheater can create and climb wispy steps made of shadow. While in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409), a torcheater gains a fly Speed equal to its Speed. If it's airborne and enters an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), the torcheater immediately falls without taking falling damage.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Shadowy Witnesses</title>

A torcheater's first glimpse of the world is whatever crime created it. Most of these spirits treat the event as inspiration, teaching them that tormenting others is acceptable. However, a placated torcheater might provide unique testimony about what they witnessed, helping investigators track down an otherwise unstoppable criminal.
</aside>
name: Torcheater

markdown:
Reality is imperfect, yet many mortals focus on their world's virtues while refusing to address its failures. A pyrkaion believes mortal delusion is the last wall propping up a broken universe, and wisdom is the pickaxe that will tear it down. Pyrkaions vacillate between teaching mortals enough to wither from indulgent self-awareness and shredding their students' institutions through insidious interrogation that leads to infighting as the asura watches. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Pyrkaion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3954) **Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea ](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 80 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117); telepathy 60 feet **Skills** [Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +18 **HP** 132 **Immunities** [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 8 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 8 **Aura of Sophistry** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 40 feet. The pyrkaion hones others' thoughts, enabling extraordinary reasoning or ruinous revelation. If the pyrkaion has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait (see Glorious Visage below), all allied creatures within the aura gain a +1 status bonus to check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 4 to mental damage. If the pyrkaion has the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait, all unallied creatures in the area take a –1 penalty to checks to Recall Knowledge and gain the effects of the [Dubious Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=5142) feat when Recalling Knowledge; whenever an affected creature Recalls Knowledge, they become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 and gain weakness 4 to mental damage for 1d4 rounds.
**Witty Reversal** ([linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet uses a linguistic or mental effect; **Effect** The pyrkaion attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the effect (counteract rank 4, counteract modifier +16). If successful, the pyrkaion can choose to corrupt the effect, dealing 2d6 persistent mental damage to the triggering effect's targets unless those targets succeed at a DC 26 Will save. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Melee** tusk +18 ([Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing **Ranged** riddle +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8 mental plus 1d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Glorious Visage** The asura sanctifies themselves as either [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), gaining the trait corresponding to their choice and losing the opposing trait; their strikes, spells, and abilities also gain the trait corresponding to their choice. The asura also gains weakness 8 to the opposing sanctification and loses any weakness to its chosen sanctification. The choice is permanent until the asura uses this ability to change their sanctification. **Insidious Query** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) The pyrkaion questions a creature within 40 feet, who must attempt a DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature becomes immune for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** If the creature doesn't [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) by the end of its next turn, it becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but until the end of their next turn, the creature also takes 2d6 mental damage when Recalling Knowledge.
Philosophical Zeal With each philosophical victory, these asuras increasingly wrestle with their own thoughts, recognizing that they're as vulnerable to selfdelusion as their victims. This can drive a pyrkaion to ravenous fits of reading and research before jealously sequestering the texts. Sometimes a pyrkaion with an inflated ego instead seeks and loses debates, craving the sting of humility to recenter their thoughts.
name: Pyrkaion

markdown:
Amneros, the underworld that houses the spirits of those who die in Iblydos, is a home to countless shades. While there, the memories of their lives are slowly washed away until they completely forget who they were. It's only at this point the spirits fade from Amneros and join the River of Souls as they head to [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) judgment. Most of the shades in the underworld grimly accept this reality. They spend their days exploring the vast city, speaking with other shades who share their predicament, and eventually come to terms with the end of their mortal lives. Those few who lived exemplary lives are granted access to the Glades of Euphoria and experience something close to paradise. Others who spent their mortal days performing evil deeds find themselves suffering punishment in Bothrios until they can be released into the city of Amnaia. A few, however, are so filled with dread at the loss of their memories that they refuse to acquiesce to such a fate. These desperate shades spend much of their time in the Marshes of Memory, a quagmire of tangled echoes of the past that have been washed away by the moat surrounding Amnaia. Lurking in the marshes, the dead spirits frantically search for their own washed-away memories, but occasionally they attack newcomers to the underworld in hopes of stealing new memories to fill the holes in their own existence. Even if stolen memories are incongruent with their own lived experience, having something to recall is better than annihilation. These shades are dubbed “forgotten dead” by the other residents and are spoken of in guarded whispers. The forgotten dead often find themselves captured by the guardians employed in Amneros and sent to Bothrios as punishment. Nevertheless, there are tales of forgotten dead who've become so successful at stealing the memories of others that they stave off rejoining the River of Souls for hundreds, even thousands, of years. **[Recall Knowledge - Shade](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Forgotten Dead](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3955) **Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea ](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 81 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +14 **Will** +9 **HP** 65 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning plus memory lapse **Memory Lapse** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Effect** The touch of a forgotten dead pulls away gossamer strands of their victim's memories. The target must attempt a DC 19 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is stupefied 2 for 1 round, then stupefied 1 for 1 minute. Once the creature has become stupefied 1, the target can attempt another saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the stupefied condition, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target cannot attempt saving throws to end the condition.
Merchants Of Memories Rumors speak of forgotten dead who collect memories from the marshes and resell them to other shades in exchange for favors. Some powerful hero-gods or practitioners of magic able to travel to the underworld actively seek out such merchants in the hopes of uncovering longlost magic or ancient secrets. In the Koinon, a carefully guarded museum in Bailax, there sits a small silver coffer with pearl runes that reputedly holds a number of memories gained in just such a fashion.
name: Forgotten Dead

<title level="1" pfs="">[Gigantic Bee Borer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3956)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Parasites come in a wide variety of types and are usually highly specialized. Bee borers are one such parasite. An infestation of bee borers can cause the collapse of a honeybee colony within a single season, devastating the harvest and local economy. Occasionally, however, some magical mishap transforms these animals into much larger, aggressive versions. With bigger bodies to feed, these parasites are soon forced to feed upon any prey they can latch onto.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea
](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 82

**Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), scent (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40

</row>

**Immunities**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
chelicerae +12,
**Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus attach

**Attach**   When a bee borer hits a target larger than itself, its legs attach it to that creature. While attached, the bee borer moves with the creature. The bee borer and the creature it's attached to are both [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). The borer remains attached until the creature dies or [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 19).

**Drain Fat** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The bee borer is attached to a living creature. **Effect** The bee borer uses its chelicerae to suck fat from the creature it's attached to. This deals 1d8+4 damage. A creature that has its fat drained by a blood borer is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 until it receives healing (of any kind or amount).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Other Giant Parasites</title>

With the sudden infusion of mythic energy in Iblydos, the danger of giant versions of parasitic creatures is a very real threat. Increased size usually means increased appetite, and such creatures pose a danger not just to adventurers but livestock as well. Giant ticks, for instance, have long been a danger in the deepest wilderness, and now there are reports of housecat-sized fleas and lice.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Gigantic Bee Drone](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3957)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Honeybee drones have a single role in most hives: to mate with a queen. Gigantic bee drones, however, also work as defenders of the nest. Although they lack stingers, their mythic mandibles can do remarkable damage.

<column gap="tiny">

mandibles +12,
**Damage** 2d8+5 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Lift and Drop** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** A Medium or smaller creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in the gigantic bee drone's mandibles; **Effect** The gigantic bee drone [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to its Speed, taking the creature with it, then drops the creature, who takes falling damage as normal.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Honey And Medicine</title>

The honey produced by bees possesses several properties that make it useful for the treatment of wounds. It helps prevent infection and inflammation, and many folk remedies make use of it. The honey of gigantic bees, being infused with mythic power, is an even more potent healing aid. A character who manages to get their hands on such a substance heals 1 extra point of damage when using it as part of a successful [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) skill check.
</aside>
