Aqudels are obsessed with control. They consider everything around them open to manipulation, and their position in the alghollthu hierarchy leads them to lord over networks of ugothols or small, established societies that they often bully and exploit from the shadows. Aqudels answer only to vidileths, following their commands with a combination of simpering compliance and scheming distrust. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Aqudel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4024) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 22 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +11 **Will** +16 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **HP** 120 --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 60 feet **Melee** tentacle +16 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning **Melee** cirrus +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +17 - **2nd** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (at will) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (×3), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (×3) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Barbed Cirri** The aqudel makes up to four cirrus Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the aqudel's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after they makes all of their attacks. **Strobe** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The aqudel changes the intensity and pattern of their skin in a rapid pulse, attempting to disorient any creatures that can see them. Creatures within 30 feet of the aqudel must attempt a DC 25 Will save. The aqudel can't use Strobe again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is dazzled for 1 minute and [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
**Critical Failure** The target is dazzled for 1 minute and stunned 2.
Alghollthu Hierarchy [Vidileths](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2813) are the leaders of the [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358), subtly influencing events to foster what they consider the most favorable outcome, and some of their plots are centuries in the making. They control vast networks of their kin, directing them to influence lower alghollthu and leverage their knowledge to manipulate lesser beings than themselves (which is how they see all others). Aqudels and nymoluses fill the role of mid-ranking administrators, taking orders from veiled masters and then enacting their plans with resources derived from their own networks. An aqudel might supervise only one small cell, while a nymolus might oversee a dozen.
name: Aqudel

markdown:
Alghollthus share a genetic memory and are theoretically capable of recalling every experience dating back to the existence of the first alghollthu hundreds of millions of years ago. Accessing these memories all at once would be lethally taxing to most alghollthus, but nymoluses are different. They revel in tapping into this genetic memory and add to it by stealing memories from others, turning them into memory crystals. A single nymolus can store up to 10 crystals at a time within their brain, replacing more mundane ones as they fill this storage and keeping the rest in collections among their lairs. To collect memories, nymoluses venture further from the seas than some of their siblings by establishing symbiotic links with ugothols. They're able to coordinate with these creatures by reshaping their bodies, then compressing and interweaving their flesh into the ugothols, which allows them to search for rare and intriguing recollections. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Nymolus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4025) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 23 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Alghollthu](/Languages.aspx?ID=24), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Inhabit Ugothol** The nymolus spends 10 minutes with a willing [ugothol](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2812) to become enmeshed with the creature. The nymolus then fully inhabits the ugothol’s body. While inhabited, the ugothol retains its mental abilities, but its body is completely controlled by the nymolus. The nymolus and ugothol each have an initiative count as if they were separate creatures. The nymolus can’t use any ability that relies on their physical form while inhabiting the ugothol, but the nymolus can still perceive and cast spells normally. The two remain enmeshed until the nymolus uses the ugothol’s Assume Form ability to separate them, or until the ugothol is slain. If the ugothol dies, the nymolus becomes [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 3 due to the force of the separation.
**Replace Recollection** The nymolus can spend 10 minutes to replace any memory with another memory they have access to via a memory crystal **Items** 3 or more memory crystals --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +15 **Will** +22 **HP** 190 --- **Speed** 15 feet, swim 60 feet **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +21 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) - **1st** [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603) (at will) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) (×3) - **5th** [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) (×3), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604) - **6th** [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) (×3) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Extract Memory** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental **Requirements** The target creature must be [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77)](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Effect** The nymolus reaches into an adjacent creature's memory and extracts up to 10 minutes of the target's experiences, forming them into a memory crystal within the nymolus's brain. The target creature must attempt a DC 27 Will save to avoid losing its memory.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature loses the target memory.
**Failure** The creature loses the target memory and is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature can only roll to end this condition if it takes damage from the nymolus themself or if [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) by an effect of at least 13th level or 7th rank. **Stunning Pulse** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The nymolus emits a powerful mental pulse that overwhelms nearby creatures. Creatures in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 27 Fortitude save to avoid being [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93). The nymolus can't use Stunning Pulse again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is stunned 1.
**Failure** The creature is stunned 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 3.
name: Nymolus

Sawed and axe-hewn timber grind together as an ambush copse moves, smashing through the forest. Cuts and burns mar the bark of this gigantic, angry mass of whirling, deadly logs. The forest never forgets. It remembers the hatchets, the rasping saws, and the smoke of crackling wood fires. It remembers the carts taking ancient trees away to cut, shape, and burn. Fey meddling or errant elemental magic draw such memories out alongside the rage and sorrow of the forest to form an ambush copse. An ambush copse exacts its ire on villages along the woodcutter's path or waits for intruders to step into its forest domain. While an ambush copse might be mistaken for a wounded arboreal, it can cease movement to appear as an overgrown pile of logs or a partially collapsed hovel. There, it waits for retribution. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Ambush Copse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4026) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 24 **Perception** +20; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +22 **Will** +22 **HP** 300 **Resistances** bludgeoning 10, piercing 10 **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15 **Berserk** An ambush copse that sees fire or axes has a chance of going berserk. At the start of its turn, if it is aware of an [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) or a [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) the size of a lit [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760) or larger, the ambush copse must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or go berserk. A berserk ambush copse can't use [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) actions and wildly attacks the nearest living creature, or the nearest object if no creatures are nearby.
**Blinding Branches** **Trigger** A creature within 20 feet of the ambush copse leaves a square during a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action it's using; **Requirements** The triggering creature is in forest terrain; **Effect** The ambush copse's elemental energy animates nearby tree branches to swat at the creature's face. The triggering creature must succeed at a DC 30 Reflex save or become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
**Felling Ambush** **Trigger** A creature moves within 10 feet of the ambush copse; **Requirements** The ambush copse is disguised as trees or logs; **Effect** The ambush copse makes a log Strike against the triggering creature. If the attack hits, the creature must attempt a DC 30 Reflex save or be knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** log +27 ([Backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=545), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 3d12+14 bludgeoning plus pounding smash **Ranged** caber +25 ([thrown 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 3d12+6 bludgeoning **Feign Copse** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until the next time it acts, the ambush copse appears to be a harmless patch of trees or logs. It has an automatic result of 43 (45 in forests) on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as trees or logs. **Pounding Smash** Regardless of whether the Strike hits or misses, the ambush copse's melee Strikes create a 5-foot-square of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) in the target's space. **Pulverizing Barrage** The ambush copse makes three log Strikes, each at a –2 penalty, all targeting the same creature. The ambush copse's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it has made all three attacks. The ambush copse gains the [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) condition until the beginning of its next turn. **Sunder Objects** When an ambush copse damages an item or structure, it deals an additional 15 damage to that item or structure.
Ravages Of Revenge An endless wait for revenge invariably leaves an ambush copse riddled with mushrooms and termites. Apply the [weak adjustment](/Rules.aspx?ID=791) to such an ambush copse and give it weakness 10 to bludgeoning damage. During a period of dormancy, parts of the ambush corpse might break off, gain their own sentience, and make their way into a nearby camp or village. Use the stats for a [twigjack](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3222) to represent these stray pieces, whose arrival might presage the full awakening of an ambush copse.
name: Ambush Copse

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Amphisbaena](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4027)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An amphisbaena is an exceedingly aggressive, venomous viper of remarkable size that bears two heads—one on each end of its body. It moves in a manner similar to the more common sidewinder snake, throwing its body forward in a loop and anchoring itself by keeping one head or the other on the ground at all times. An amphisbaena can grow up to 14 feet in length and weigh as much as 250 pounds.

 Amphisbaenas' typical prey include rabbits, foxes, various birds, small deer, and even humanoids if presented the opportunity. Fiercely territorial by nature, they attack just about anything that draws near their lairs regardless of the intruder's size. Many a child has been warned about going too far into the woods alone, lest they wander close to an amphisbaena's den and become the creature's next meal.

 The venom of an amphisbaena is incredibly potent, able to take down a stout [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) within minutes if left untreated. It also has uses in a variety of healing remedies, however, which makes it a valuable commodity if collected. For example, a pregnant person might be advised to drink the venom in small, dilute doses to help safeguard the pregnancy. Mixing small amounts of the venom with various herbs and oil creates a poultice that dulls aches and pains. As a result, the image of an amphisbaena appears in many contexts associated with healing or alchemical subjects, such as labels for tinctures, annotations in herbalists' records, and illustrations in textbooks of medicine.

 The first amphisbaena is said to have formed from the blood that fell as a [medusa's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3096) head was severed. This story's origin most likely stems from the fact that amphisbaenas are oddly immune to petrification, which in turn sometimes leads them to be kept as pets by medusas. A medusa might regard such a viper as their most treasured companion or perhaps even as their own child. Despite these fabled supernatural origins and the occasional special treatment they receive, though, amphisbaenas are entirely mundane animals with only rudimentary intelligence and no innate magical abilities whatsoever.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Amphisbaena Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round (1 round)

**Blinding Spittle**   A creature critically hit by an amphisbaena's spit Strike is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.

**Twin Bites** <actions string="Single Action" />  An amphisbaena makes a fangs Strike with each of its heads, each against a different target. Both Strikes count toward its multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after it has made both attacks.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Amphisbaena Variants</title>

While the two-headed snake is by far the most common amphisbaena, the term sometimes describes other creatures with heads on both ends of their bodies. Occasionally, stories circulate of a monstrous amphisbaena with the body of a lizard, clawed feet, or even feathered wings. These stories are typically chalked up to an overactive imagination or an abundance of drink, but they persist regardless.
</aside>
name: Amphisbaena

Kuribus serve as the guardians of temples and other holy sites. They are humanoid in appearance and roughly the size of human children. Their bodies look like stone, which allows kuribus to disguise themselves as statues for extended periods of time (sometimes up to centuries). When defending their sites, kuribus harry invaders from a distance. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Kuribu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4028) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 26 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 45 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 5 **Sentinel's Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 30 feet. The kuribu and any other creature in the aura defending the same holy site gain a +1 status bonus to AC. This aura is suppressed while Statue is in effect.
**Immobilizing Ambush** **Requirements** The kuribu is disguised as a statue; **Trigger** A creature moves within 60 feet of the kuribu; **Effect** The kuribu springs into action by making a shortbow Strike against the triggering creature. If the Strike hits, the creature is pinned by the arrow, as described in the [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) critical specialization. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +11 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing **Blessed Aspect** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The kuribu's face transforms and their holy countenance unleashes an attack based on the aspect the kuribu chooses. The kuribu can't use Blessed Aspect again for 1d4 rounds. They can revert back to their humanoid appearance at any time but they still have to wait before using Blessed Aspect again.
  • **Eagle** The kuribu unleashes a disorienting screech in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that deals 2d10 sonic damage with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. A creature that critically fails is also [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
  • **Lion** The kuribu makes a powerful jaws Strike against an adjacent creature. The attack has a +12 attack modifier and deals 4d6 piercing damage plus 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
  • **Ox** The kuribu charges into a creature. The kuribu [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) or Strides. At the end of their movement dealing 4d6 bludgeoning damage to it with a DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. If the target critically fails, it is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Statue** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until the next time they act, the kuribu appears to be a statue. They have an automatic result of 29 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to pass as a statue.
Holy Countenances Kuribus can partially take on the appearance of powerful animals and tap into those creatures' abilities in combat. Though most kuribus manifest the animals featured here, some instead tap into the abilities of other creatures like elephants or wolves. Some divine accounts speak of ancient kuribus who can transform entirely into these creatures and remain vigilant for decades in the guise of animal statues.
name: Kuribu

Celestial artisans and elegant dancers called tennins serve as [Nirvana's](/Planes.aspx?ID=44) attendants, where they weave the gods' marvelous, seamless garments from clouds and frost and entertain the courts with skyward dances, their footsteps lighter than dew. During catastrophic wars and disasters, tennins descend from Nirvana to mend what is broken and soothe those who suffer. The realm of Nirvana often promotes the virtuous souls of dutiful craftspeople and dedicated musicians to the ranks of the brocade angels so they might use their talents to bring beauty and kindness to a [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) overwhelmed by war. It feels like there have always been too few tennins to attend to the needs of an entire world. Tragically, their numbers are dwindling in the wake of [Gorum's](/Deities.aspx?ID=283) death, which has sparked and renewed numerous conflicts. Many angels have fallen or lost their way amid the discord. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Tennin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4029) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 26 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), fingers upon the loom ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 60 feet **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Fingers Upon the Loom** The tennin can detect creatures wearing or holding any clothing as a precise sense with a range of 60 feet.
**Flawless Celestial Shawl** Each tennin has a unique flawless celestial shawl (see below). A tennin without their flawless celestial shawl can't use their fly Speed or cast divine innate spells.
**Secrets of Immortality** The tennin can use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) instead of [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396), [Treat Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=2397), [Treat Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=2398), and [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399). **Items** [_flawless celestial shawl_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4048), _[+1 striking handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +21 **Will** +17 **HP** 155 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 10 **Five Color Dance** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 30 feet. Any piece of clothing or equipment worn or held by an ally within the aura has its Hardness increased by 3, Hit Points increased by 20, and Broken Threshold increased by 10. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet; dancer across the heavens, [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** _fist_ +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)), **Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning plus 2d6 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673) - **2nd** [Peaceful Rest](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625) (at will) - **4th** [Sure Footing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1708) (at will) - **5th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659), [Wall of Ice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1750) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Dancer Across the Heavens** The tennin can Step into [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and can use either their land or fly Speed to Step. Also, when the tennin attempts a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check to dance, they can Step or Stride as a free action.
Tragedies Of Two Worlds Brocade angels often become entangled in romantic relations with mortals, especially with virtuous individuals who share similarly lofty goals. These affairs rarely go well, as the endless duties of [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) take precedence over the joys and comforts of the heart. Still, some tennins have been known to discard their _[flawless celestial shawls](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4048)_ to stay with their lovers. Darker tales also exist of unscrupulous mortals overcome by desire or greed, who steal these shawls to force the angels to stay with or serve them.
name: Tennin

markdown:
Sramanas, or renouncer angels, exist to help liberate the penitent and ease their suffering. Unusual for angels of such power, sramanas are drawn exclusively from compassionate and enlightened mortal souls who are often more perceptive to the pains of mortal existence. These tireless celestials embark upon impossible tasks and unfathomable labors to free souls lost, abandoned, and imprisoned in the Lower Planes. This earns them great enmity from fiends and other callous entities, many of whom treat souls as simple playthings and currency and who ironically see the renouncer angels as thieves of souls they've “rightfully” cheated, stolen, or trafficked for their own purposes. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Sramana](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4030) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 28 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), heed the fettered ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 120 feet **Languages** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +29, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +26, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +29 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +5 **Heed the Fettered** The sramana can detect penitent creatures who wish to atone for their misdeeds, creatures with the [soulbound](/Traits.aspx?ID=773) trait, and soul gems as an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense with a range of 120 feet.
**Soul-Rescuing Vow** A sramana can use [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) to teleport near a truly penitent creature, soulbound creature, or soul gem of which they're aware. If they do, they don't need a planar key and arrive 1d20 miles away from the subject. They can also teleport to [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44) or The [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) without a planar key. **Items** _[+1 striking handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086)_, [expanded healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=59), _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [khakkara](/Weapons.aspx?ID=126)_ --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +24 **Will** +28 **HP** 300 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 15 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 15 **Aura of Renunciation** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet. Truly penitent creatures in the sramana's aura are affected by a DC 35 [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) spell. If any creature within the aura takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), _sanctuary_ ends for only that creature, not for the other creatures in the aura.
In addition, soul gems in the aura can't be ingested, consumed, or otherwise used. A creature who attempts to do so becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) unless it succeeds at a DC 37 Fortitude save. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 75 feet **Melee** _khakkara_ +31 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=718), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+14 bludgeoning plus 2d6 spirit **Melee** _fist_ +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 2d4+14 bludgeoning plus 2d6 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 37, attack +29 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **7th** [Calm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; see soul-rescuing vow), [Planar Seal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) - **8th** [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [Divine Inspiration](/Spells.aspx?ID=1497), [Moment of Renewal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1607), [Pinpoint](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 37 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) - **4th** [Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) **Shelter the Suffering** **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The sramana tosses the shawl of their robes into the air, where it expands to protect the suffering. Each truly penitent creature in a 100-foot emanation is affected by an [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) spell, and the area is affected by the [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526) spell, though it affects only penitent creatures and [soulbound](/Traits.aspx?ID=773) creatures. These effects last for 1 round but can be sustained for up to 1 hour.
Soul Saviors Over the course of helping souls renounce suffering, many sramanas develop alliances with [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458), whose goal of returning the lost and trapped to the River of Souls they gladly share. These bonds run so deep that it isn't unusual to find sramana volunteering as legal advocates in the [Boneyard's](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) courts.
name: Sramana

markdown:
When once-honorable mortals fall to wickedness and perform terrible atrocities, ninkondas set out to track them down. Clad in plate armor that features a brilliantly shining mirror in the chest and bearing spikes and nails impaled into their bodies, ninkondas serve as hunters for celestial forces. Rather than immediately slaying their quarries, however, ninkondas do their best to encourage a change of heart and foster eventual redemption in their targets. Ninkondas use their mirrors to reflect the sins of the target and showcase the state of their soul, and many targets seek to change after glimpsing this reflection. Those who don't soon find themselves facing off against the wordless might of their hunter. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Ninkonda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4031) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 29 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +26 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +7 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +27 **Will** +33 **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 15 **Aura of Reflection** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 60 feet. The ninkonda's mirror reflects the weaknesses in creatures' souls. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 36 Will save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and take a –2 penalty to Will saves against the ninkonda's abilities for 1 round.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Reflect Spell** **Trigger** The ninkonda is targeted by a ranged spell attack; **Effect** The ninkonda attempts to reflect the spell with the mirror in their armor. They gain a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, the spell is reflected back at the caster, who must roll a second ranged spell attack against their own AC to determine if the spell hits them instead. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 45 feet **Melee** nailed fist +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+17 bludgeoning plus 2d8 piercing **Ranged** nail blast +34 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+8 piercing plus 2d8 persistent bleed **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **3rd** [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only) - **8th** [Pinpoint](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Rituals** DC 38 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) - **4th** [Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) **Nail Barrage** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The ninkonda sprays a mass of nails that deal 14d8 piercing damage in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. They can't use Nail Barrage again for 1d4 rounds. **Soul Reflection** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The ninkonda aims the mirror in their armor at a creature [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) by their aura of reflection to force the creature to gaze upon its past sins. The creature must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round as it reflects upon its actions (or 1 minute on a critical failure, as the creature's actions continue to weigh on it). A [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creature or a morally upstanding creature (as determined by the GM) uses the outcome that is one degree of success better than it rolled on its save. A creature that fails its save is then temporarily immune to Soul Reflection for 1 minute.
Seeking Redemption Ninkondas have a particularly unique origin among angels. While the occasional ninkonda is born from the soul of a righteous mortal who sought to continue their work of redeeming others in the afterlife, most stem from the ranks of the less scrupulous. After centuries or millennia of perseverance, souls seeking true redemption earn the ability to join the celestial ranks of angels, usually as cassisians or other lowly beings. As they rise through the celestial ranks, they gravitate toward the role of ninkondas in hopes of helping others. In a way, ninkondas are self-perpetuating. Given enough time, they could amass countless angels within their ranks thanks to their own efforts.
name: Ninkonda

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Bloodsiphon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4033) **Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 23 **Perception** +10; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +7 **Will** +11 **HP** 80 (negative healing) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Death Burst** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When the bloodsiphon dies, its body explodes in a cloudy red burst of necrotic dried blood in a 20-foot area. Creatures in range must attempt a DC 21 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 negative damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6 negative damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d6 negative damage
and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 for 1 hour. --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** mouth +14, **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus Grab **Ranged** spittle +11 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6 negative **Blood Drain** **Requirements** The bloodsiphon has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The bloodsiphon drains blood from the creature it has grabbed. This deals 4d4 damage and the bloodsiphon grows temporarily moist and slimy as it regains 8 Hit Points, gaining any excess as [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute. A creature that has its blood drained by the bloodsiphon is drained 1 until it receives any type of healing.
name: Bloodsiphon

- **1st**

**Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** Nhakazarin is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour; **Effect** Nhakazarin devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 3d6 Hit Points. Nhakazarin can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Consume Masterpiece** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** Nhakazarin is adjacent to the rotting statue of Belcorra in area **C34**; Effect Nhakazarin devours a chunk of flesh from the statue and regains 3d6 Hit Points and is quickened 1 for 1 round. She can use this extra action only to make a jaws Strike or Cast a Spell. She can feed from the statue only once each day.

**Ghoul Fever**  ([disease Saving Throw DC 22 Fortitude](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effects (1 day); **Stage 2** 2d6 negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 2d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a [ghoul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=218) the next midnight

**Paralysis**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living, non-elf creature hit by a attack from Nhakazarin must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each save.

**Swift Leap** <actions string="Single Action" />  Nhakazarin jumps up to half her Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

</column>
name: Nhakazarin

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Volluk Azrinae](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4042) **Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 54 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 25 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 85 (fast healing 7) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** physical 7 **Weaknesses** area damage 7, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 7 **Discorporate** As worm that walks, but slow-moving leeches give the heroes 2 rounds, rather than 1 round, to dispatch the fleeing vermin before they escape
**Hateful Memories** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) If Volluk can see an accurate depiction of his former appearance as a living drow at the start of his turn, he must attempt a DC 28 Will save or become filled with self-loathing and become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round. --- **Speed** 10 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** tendril +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+8 persistent piercing damage **Rituals** - **4th** [Animate Object](/Rituals.aspx?ID=107), [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117) **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Comprehend Language](/Spells.aspx?ID=46), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Vomit Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=726) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) (4 slots) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693), [Worm's Repast](/Spells.aspx?ID=839) (3 slots) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Faerie Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=107) (at will) **Squirming Embrace** Volluk [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), ending his movement sharing a space with a creature, and deals 3d8 piercing damage to the creature. The creature can attempt a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. **Swarm Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Volluk collapses into a shapeless swarm of leeches. He drops all held, worn, and carried items. While discorporated, he can't use attack actions and can't cast spells, but he can move through areas small enough for his individual leeches to fit without having to Squeeze. He can use the same action to coalesce back into his normal form.
name: Volluk Azrinae

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 24, attack +15
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330)
- **1st**
[Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140), [Magic Fang](/Spells.aspx?ID=179)
- **2nd**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=316), [Water Breathing](/Spells.aspx?ID=370)
- **3rd**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366)

**Animal Order Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 24
- **3rd**
[Heal Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=474)

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Jaul changes into his humanoid, hybrid, or animal shape. Each shape has a specific, persistent appearance. Jaul's natural form is its hybrid shape. In humanoid shape, Jaul uses its original humanoid size, loses his jaws and claws Strikes, and gains a melee fist Strike that deals bludgeoning damage equal to the slashing damage dealt by its claw. In animal shape, his Speed and size change to that of the animal, it gains any special Strike effects of the animal that it didn't already have (such as [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)), and it loses his weapon Strikes. human with fist +16 for 1d6+7 bludgeoning, or wolf with Speed 40 feet and jaws with [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46).

**Curse of the Werewolf**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) This curse only affects [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=628); **Saving Throw** DC 23 Fortitude. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into a werewolf until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious at dawn.

**Moon Frenzy**  ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a full moon appears in the night sky, Jaul must enter his hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes Large, increases his reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of his jaws by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, Jaul returns to humanoid form and is fatigued for 2d4 hours.

**Wolf Coordination**   Jaul's Strikes deal 1d6 extra damage to creatures within his wolf's reach.

</column>
name: Jaul Mezmin

markdown:
Otari Ilvashti recalls little of his life. His time as a child growing up in Absalom and his adventures with the other members of the Roseguard have dwindled to distant but pleasant memories. His fear of the devastation Belcorra plans to unleash upon Absalom is what keeps him shackled to this world as a ghost. When the Roseguard fought Belcorra in Gauntlight Keep, she unleashed a powerful magical explosion that collapsed the room where the fight took place. Belcorra and three heroes avoided the collapse and fought on— to Belcorra's ultimate defeat—but the rogue Otari was caught beneath the collapse. His companions assumed he was pulverized, but Otari had fallen into a level beneath Gauntlight Keep that none of the Roseguard knew existed. Otari desperately wanted to escape to the surface and share his discovery, but Belcorra's minions forced him to flee ever deeper into the vaults, until finally he was cornered in a dead-end cave by an immense otyugh that left him trapped and suffering from filth fever. Otari died in that deep cave, but his ghost lingers on. ** Campaign role** Otari's role is to present the tasks necessary to reach the lower levels of the Abomination Vaults described in [Pathfinder Adventure Path #164: Hands of the Devil](/Sources.aspx?ID=75). Once the heroes encounter Otari's ghost and drive off the wisps that torment him, he relates his tale of woe. He has difficulty remembering much of his life, but becomes very emotional if he learns that the Roseguard survived and named their town after him. While Otari's information about the dungeon is centuries out of date, his supernatural link to the Abomination Vaults affords him glimpses into the inner workings of the dungeon and its key players. As Otari speaks to the heroes, make sure he conveys the following key points of information to them, either in the form of answers to questions or volunteered information.
  • Belcorra harbored a grudge against Absalom and intended to use her sinister lighthouse, Gauntlight, as a weapon against the city. The Roseguard never discovered its actual use, other than that it had something to do with the ability to transport creatures across significant distances and could animate the dead with its light. None of the other Roseguard members knew about the Abomination Vaults beneath Gauntlight. Otari learned about them only in his last few days alive.
  • Some property of the Abomination Vaults increases the manifestation of ghosts, spectral undead, and haunts within their domain. Otari initially believed this was a side effect from Gauntlight, but has now come to suspect a link to a much more ancient and ominous source deep below this level. He knows nothing of Nhimbaloth, but if the heroes tell him of the Outer God, he suspects her influence is the source.
  • Belcorra herself is now a ghost, although she cannot move far from the site to which she is anchored. Otari can sense her somewhere deep underground and is also aware that the range of her movements is slowly expanding.
  • Otari suspects Belcorra intends to fully reactivate Gauntlight and use it to begin her long-delayed attack on Absalom. She must be put to rest before she can finish this task. Otari isn't sure how to stop her, but believes that clues await discovery in the deeper levels of the Abomination Vaults.
  • Finally, Otari feels the barrier in area D12 blocking progress deeper into the Abomination Vaults. He understands that it is powered by the souls of Belcorra's defeated enemies. Fortunately, he also understands how to take the barrier down. The Roseguard were the adventurers who finally killed her, and their psychic resonance can drop the barrier. The heroes must find and place four icons the Roseguard once held dear onto the altar in area D13 to do this. Otari's thieves' tools are here with his bones and serve as his icon. The other icons are the brooch belonging to the cleric Aesephna Menhemes (which bears the religious symbol of her god, Erastil), the spellbook belonging to the wizard Zarmavdian, and the trusty longsword of the fighter Vol Rajani.
Once the heroes take on the burden of facing and defeating Belcorra and open the way to the deeper levels of the Abomination Vaults, Otari's soul is free to travel to its final reward. You should ensure he has imparted all of the above information to the heroes before he fades away. If you wish, though, you can put a darker spin on things—rather than have Otari's spirit achieve freedom, it could instead be captured by the temple of [Nhimbaloth](/Deities.aspx?ID=572) at the bottom of the Abomination Vaults. In this case, the heroes should learn about Otari's fate through a vision or nightmare, or perhaps even see his tormented ghost pulled downward against his will. Otari's ghost remains trapped until the heroes finally defeat Belcorra. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 36 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Otari Ilvashti](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4045) **Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 88 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=159) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +20 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Site Bound** Otari can stray only a short distance from where he was killed or the place it haunts (area **D18**). --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +20 **Will** +17 **HP** 120 (negative healing, rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** ; double resistance vs. nonmagical) **Rejuvenation** Securing the four icons of the Roseguard and placing them on the altar in area **D13** allows Otari to move on to the afterlife. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d6+6 negative **Filth Fever** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) The sickened and unconscious conditions from filth fever don't improve on their own until the disease is cured. **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1d4 hours); **Stage 2** [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** sickened 1 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as long as the affected creature remains sickened (1 day); Stage 4 [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) (1 day); **Stage 5** dead **Infested Shadow** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) Otari fades away, momentarily emerges from the shadow of another creature within 30 feet, and makes a ghostly hand Strike against a single target within reach of this creature (but not against the creature whose shadow he infests). On a successful Strike, the target is also [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of Otari's next turn. Otari then reappears at his starting point. The creature whose shadow Otari infests is exposed to filth fever. **Sneak Attack** Otari deals 2d6 extra precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Otari Ilvashti

creature_ability: Blood Magic When Wrin casts   charm  ,   enthrall  ,   faerie dust  ,   ghost sound  , or   hideous laughter  , fey glamours dance around Wrin or one target of the spell, causing them to be  concealed  for 1 round. Such obvious concealment can't be used to Hide.
markdown:
Wrin Sivinxi grew up in the small village of Glitterbough in Kyonin, but a traveler wouldn't find her hometown if they were to visit that nation today, for Glitterbough was destroyed 10 years ago by demons in the service of [Treerazer](/Deities.aspx?ID=324). Despite the loss of her hometown, Wrin feels no sadness or despair about the event, for none perished in Glitterbough's fall. In the days before the demon army's attack, Wrin had a vision of the disaster. She warned her parents and neighbors, and their trust in her visions gave the town the time it needed to relocate everyone in the village to safety. When the demons came, they found Glitterbough deserted, and when they burned it in frustration, they suffered greatly from the magical traps the elves had left behind to greet them. When she came of age a few years later, Wrin left her parents and friends, eager to find her own place in the world. As she traveled, she trusted in her visions and her unusual faith in a pantheon of deities associated with space and time she refers to as the [Cosmic Caravan](/Deities.aspx?ID=377), looking to the nightly stars to make decisions for tomorrow. Her knack for finding lost things and gathering strange treasures made establishing her own curio shop an obvious choice, and it wasn't by chance that she decided to set up that shop in Otari—it was simply the port she'd arrived at when her capacity to carry her collection finally outweighed its portability. She opened her shop, Wrin's Wonders, 4 years ago. In that time, she's become part of the community, even if many of its citizens regard her as a good-natured eccentric. Sales of the strange and bizarre aren't brisk in the small town, but that's no matter to Wrin—she's patient and has enjoyed her time in Otari so far. One day, she hopes to make enough money to fund a trip west, for she's heard tell of an abandoned observatory in the small town of Crookcove on Ravounel's southwestern coast that she hopes to purchase and transform into a temple devoted to the Cosmic Caravan. But for the time being, she's content to stay in Otari and sleep under the stars. Wrin's tiefling ancestry is impossible to miss, and it's something she long ago decided to embrace. Eyes devoid of pupils, small ram horns curling around her elven ears, a thin tail, and fingertips that sparkle with motes of glittering lights leave no doubt that she is touched by supernatural forces. Yet her quick smile and welcoming attitude have always helped her to secure friendships during her travels. Campaign Use Wrin's primary purpose is to motivate the heroes' initial explorations and to give them a focal point to gather around. The Abomination Vaults Player's Guide assumes that Wrin is a contact the heroes share in common, so when she notices a strange buildup of eerie light atop distant Gauntlight, it's natural for her to confide her fears in the heroes. As the campaign progresses, Wrin can help the heroes in other ways, such as by performing astrology readings for the heroes to aid them in their explorations. While she won't give her store's wares away for free, her establishment is the best place to buy and sell the odd magical treasures heroes tend to find and crave. Wrin can also introduce the heroes to the wonders of the spirit world—her tutelage unlocks the [ghost hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=88) archetype. Despite the claustrophobia she suffers, if the heroes find themselves in over their head in the Abomination Vaults, you can have Wrin face her fears to brave the dungeon and mount a rescue. Of course, you shouldn't use Wrin in this capacity except as a last resort to keep the campaign from reaching an untimely end! **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Wrin Sivinxi](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4046) **Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 90 **Perception** +12; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), spirit sight (precise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=174) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Astrology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13, [Spirit Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** harrow fortune-telling deck, [silver religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), _[+1 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399)_, violin --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 75 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** starknife +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing **Melee** tail +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Ranged** starknife +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing **Primal Spontaneous Spells** DC 22, attack +14 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Disrupt Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=79), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143), [Pest Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=217) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Remove Disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) (3 slots) **Bloodline Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 22 - **3rd** [Faerie Dust](/Spells.aspx?ID=508) **Blood Magic When Wrin casts [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [_faerie dust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2108), [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), or [_hideous laughter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), fey glamours dance around Wrin or one target of the spell, causing them to be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 round. Such obvious concealment can't be used to Hide.** **Read the Stars** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Wrin attempts to read the future in the stars by spending an hour studying the night sky—she can't use this ability during the day, on an overcast night, or when she can't otherwise study the stars. Although Wrin usually performs this reading for an individual person or to answer a pressing question she has, for the purposes of this campaign she reads the stars for the party as a whole. She attempts an [Astrology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check, using the [DC appropriate for the heroes' current level.](/Rules.aspx?ID=681) **Critical Success** The results of Wrin's astrological readings have the effects of [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647), and the heroes gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws for 24 hours.
**Success** The results of Wrin's astrological readings have the effects of [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), but can predict results up to 24 hours into the future.
**Failure** No reading.
**Critical Failure** Wrin brushes against hostile spiritual forces, and cannot Read the Stars for 1 week.
name: Wrin Sivinxi

<title level="1" pfs="">[Carman Rajani](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4048)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Carman Rajani is the last living descendant of the famous Vol Rajani, member of the Roseguard and co-founder of Otari. Carman grew up on stories of Vol's bravery, her dedication to her friends, and her skill-at-arms with her famous sword, the Cooperative Blade. As a boy, Carman didn't understand why his family's sword was hanging in the Dawnflower Library and not in their house. It was theirs, or so his parents insisted.

As Carman grew older, he first worked at Otari's blacksmith's shop, Blades for Glades, and later won the shop in a lucky wager against the prior owner. Yet he couldn't forget his family's sword. Officially, the Menhemes family claimed ownership over Vol's ancient blade but “allowed” it to stay in the library in an act of purported generosity that Carman found offensive. 

The summer his parents were waylaid and slain by bandits on the road from Absalom, Carman became a broken man. His work at the smithy became erratic, and he stopped relaxing with his friends and helping his community. With no other direction, Carman squandered his inheritance, spending the money on entertainment, fine clothes, and rare whiskey. He started spending more time gambling and drinking at the Crook's Nook than he did at his smithy. Eventually the inheritance money ran out, and Carman was faced with a sizable debt. Several owed favors later, he started grifting travelers or hustling them at the knife-throwing board at the Crook's Nook. But his vices still outpaced his earnings from petty cons, and soon he took to outright thievery under the tutelage of Yinyasmera, owner of the Crook's Nook and ringleader of the Osprey Club, Otari's thieves' guild. The “Ace of Blades” is the moniker given to the best knife thrower in the Crook's Nook, and Carman Rajani has held that title for so long that it's become his nickname among fellow members of the Osprey Club.

Within a few years, Carman had settled into his new life, working as a smith during the day, carousing at the Crook's Nook at night, and occasionally engaging in some petty larceny when his coin purse started to get light. When he heard rumors that people thought he was wasting his life, though, it stung his pride. He resolved to run for mayor against Oseph, to show the whole town that his family was the equal of the Menhemes family.

Everyone thought Carman's candidacy was a joke. The second time he ran, his platform became nothing more than sharp personal attacks against the Menhemes family; the townspeople felt the joke had gone sour and considered it a waste of their time. He met their jibes with violence, getting into brawls to prove he was the better man. The third time he ran for office, he actually put in effort to help the community; though he lost by much smaller margin, he took the loss as proof that kindness can't get you any further than your fists can. The fourth time, he tried to force a successful campaign with open bribes and blackmail.

Oseph won by a landslide, showing up on Election Day wearing the Cooperative Blade as part of his celebratory regalia. Carman finally decided that the magic sword must be the key to his success. Everyone in town knew it brought Otari good luck, so whoever held the sword had the town in their pocket. As Vol's heir, he deserved the sword. He begged Oseph to sell the weapon, but Oseph suspected it was some new scheme—or worse, connected to one of Carman's rumored crimes or debts—and countered with delays, excuses, and price increases.

Finally, Carman decided he'd had enough. If he wanted his family's sword back, he'd have to take it. He just needed to find the right opportunity. Carman is Nidalese in heritage, with pale skin, dark hair, and severe facial features. His lifetime of work at Blades for Glades has made him exceptionally muscular to supplement his natural grace, though his lifetime of hard drinking is starting to take its toll. He's a sloppy dresser who wears once-fine, sweat-stained clothes and a belt hanging with work hammers.

Carman is a bitter, hateful man. He bases his self-worth on how he compares to Vol, his famous ancestor. Since tales about her are, by now, all embellished myth, there's no way Carman can measure up. His many failings give him a sense of inadequacy that causes him to lash out at others, particularly when they're expressing doubt in his capabilities or worth. Yet Carman still thinks that as the last descendant of a Roseguard hero, he's better than everyone else in town and deserving of far more than life's given him.

<title level="2" right="">Campaign Role</title> Carman's initial role in this adventure is as a minor obstacle; the heroes need the four icons of the Roseguard, and Carman has stolen one of them, the Cooperative Blade. Tracking Carman down is one of the heroes' first tasks. They're likely to bring Carman to justice rather than kill him, as he's quick to surrender when things turn against him. Some parties might even agree that Carman's claim to the sword is valid and give the Cooperative Blade back to him.

But Carman has a greater role to play in this adventure. As the last descendant of one of the Roseguard, his soul is important to the contract devil Urevian. Urevian gives the heroes the option to bring the reprobate Carman to him, providing the key moral dilemma of this adventure. Carman's ultimate fate is in the heroes' hands.

This gas deals 7d6 negative damage to creatures in this area as their flesh curdles and rots (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).

The target must make a DC 25 Fortitude save. If the Strike was a critical hit, treat the target's save result as one degree worse. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
**Failure** The target is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is paralyzed for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, it can attempt a new Fortitude save to reduce the remaining duration by 1 round or end it entirely on a critical success. **Wicked Bite** **Requirements** Vischari damaged a creature with a jaws Strike on her last action; **Effect** Vischari maintains contact, turning the creature's flesh translucent around the site of the injury. Vischari chooses one of two options, each of which requires the target to attempt a DC 25 Fortitude save. If her jaws Strike was a critical hit, the creature suffers both effects, using the same save result for both.
  • **Drain Blood** Vischari drinks some of the creature's blood. On a failed save, the creature is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and Vischari regains 10 HP (on a critical failure, it's drained 2 and Vischari regains 20 HP).
  • **Drain Vitality** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Vischari draws out some of the creature's vital essence. The creature becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 hour on a failed save (or enfeebled 2 for 1 hour on a critical failure).

Sacuishu regains 2d8 Hit Points, and the target must attempt a DC 24 Will save. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The first time the target [Casts a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=3471) before the start of Sacuishu's next turn, the spell is disrupted unless the target succeeds at a DC 15 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333).
**Failure** As success, but the effect applies the first time the target Casts a Spell within the next minute.

She can end this effect with another use of this action. If she uses her shock attack while invisible, the arc of electricity lets any observer determine her location, making Sacuishu hidden to observers only until she moves.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animate Dream](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4057)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An animate dream coalesces from centuries of stray fragments of reverie left behind by slumbering minds drifting through the [Dimension of Dreams](/Planes.aspx?ID=47). In most cases, these fragments simply disperse on their own, but when dreamers flee from nightmares back to the waking world, they can instead take on a supernatural life of their own. They have little reasoning and intent except to seek similar fragments, absorb them, and grow. When enough of them gather together, they merge into a horrific form as an animate dream.

 Individually, animate dreams can take on a wide range of appearances, but most adopt rudimentary shapes akin to the living forms of the minds that spawned them. Animate dreams that rise from slumbering humanoids are the most commonly encountered; thusly, they appear humanoid, but with distorted, nightmarish shapes that incorporate additional twisted limbs and frightening features. In these forms, animate dreams find their way out of the Dreamlands and into the waking world, only to discover they have no way of returning and carry a relentless hunger that only new nightmares can sate.

 An animate dream thrives on the terror sparked by nightmares and uses its supernatural abilities to invoke such feelings in the minds of its victims, cursing its prey by creating a permanent link to that individual's dreams. Thereafter, the victim is subjected to all manner of recurring nightmares. As these torments continue, the victim finds it more and more difficult to remain awake and, in the end, the victim plunges into an accursed slumber from which it can't return. These victims provide the animate dream with the purest form of fear and are its preferred sustenance.

 An animate dream can subsist on the ambient fear generated by regular nightmares, but these lesser fears aren't as satisfying and often drive the animate dream to seek out new victims. Left without a source of fear, an animate dream won't starve to death, but it will grow increasingly violent and desperate. Famished animate dreams are the ones most often encountered, as they've been forced to abandon subtlety to pursue more blatant methods of harvesting fear. Yet such animate dreams also tend to be the least powerful of their kind. Those who have grown metaphysically fat on fear have generally done so by specializing in a specific nightmare theme, such as being buried alive, being swept away by ocean waves, or being consumed by wild beasts. Such animate dreams often have similarly themed innate spells to augment their powers.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Arboreal Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4062)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While some forests have an idyllic, peaceful quality to them, others feel distinctly unfriendly or even sinister—these forests are the favored haunts of arboreal reapers. Sometimes these woodlands are sought out by secretive practitioners of malicious arts who claim the unsettling ambiance aids their abilities; arboreal reapers see no reason to interfere with such practices as long as the balance of their forests isn't harmed.

 All arboreals vary in appearance based on their surroundings, but arboreal reapers always seem vaguely eerie, whether they watch over a grim weald or a tropical rainforest. Often slightly warped, twisted, and covered in spiky protrusions, arboreal reapers sometimes sprout around old ruins or other permanent structures, breaking down and incorporating the structures as they mature and become more mobile.

 Arboreal reapers focus on the essential decaying phase of a forest's life cycle, becoming especially active in autumn and winter seasons. Their influence can often be seen in the explosion of mushrooms on a rotting log or the enticing fronds of a flourishing carnivorous plant. Like arboreal wardens, these woodland guardians are quicker to react to perceived threats than the more deliberate regents or retiring archives—at least by arboreal standards.

markdown:
Arboreal archives are solitary beings typically found in the remote wilderness. These ancient monoliths hold the memories of their vast territories, keeping mental records of the passing seasons of the world around them. Their knowledge of humanoid affairs varies, as they find quickly shifting political situations difficult to comprehend, but through fungal networks, reports from arboreal wardens, and other woodland chatter, arboreal archives learn of each storm, drought, or logging threat encountered throughout their long years. They primarily observe and record rather than interfere, but they willingly offer wisdom to those who respectfully seek them out. Arboreal regents call upon archives for advice in times of strife, conferring on the best course of action to protect their realms. It's rare for there to be more than one arboreal archive in a given region. When an archive senses they're nearing the end of their considerable life, a grove of regents gathers to nominate the wisest among them to become the next archive. After four seasons of deliberation, all arboreals in the area congregate to witness the succession ritual, during which the elder arboreal archive transfers their collected wisdom to the elected replacement before retiring. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Arboreal Archive](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4063) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 34 **Perception** +25; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** -1 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +17 **Will** +27 **HP** 230 **Resistances** bludgeoning 10, piercing 10 **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15 **Abeyance Rift** If an arboreal archive dies unexpectedly before passing on their knowledge in a succession ritual, the amassed lore within their roots and boughs explodes out in a shock wave that deals 8d10 mental damage to creatures within 30 feet (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save) before dissipating; those who fail also fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** branch +25 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) (at will) - **6th** [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681) **Memory Maelstrom** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The arboreal archive tries to overwhelm foes with a surge of information they've absorbed over their long life. This surge deals 5d6 mental damage to each enemy within 40 feet, who must attempt a DC 32 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature maintains its composure, takes no damage, and is temporarily immune to Memory Maelstrom for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) and takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is stunned 3.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 2d4 rounds, and is stunned 3.
Painting Memories As with all arboreals, no two arboreal archives are identical in appearance. The uniquely patterned bark of arboreal archives makes them even more distinct from one another, painting a mysterious and unique record of their knowledge of Forest Lore. Practitioners of primal magic believe the earthy colors adorning each arboreal archive's body hold clues about the memories and myths the creature collects and preserves.
name: Arboreal Archive
